Diego Raspati is an italian guitarist and sound engineer based in London.

His passion for music began with the study of classical guitar, but quickly evolved to the electric guitar.

Moved by a profound interest for the whole music production process, he studied at the prestigious St.Luis College of Music in Rome an intensive 2 years Diploma course in Sound Engineering and Music Technology.

Shortly after completing the course, he won a Scholarship at Tech Music School (Now BIMM) in London, where he studied a Diploma in Popular Music Performance.

Diego has written monthly columns about his experience as a musician in London on the revered italian guitar magazine “Chitarre”.

He then completed a BMus in Popular Music Performance course at London College of Music where he has been mentored by top UK musicians such as: John Wheatcroft, Simon Carter (Jamiroquai), Femi Temowo (Amy Winehouse) to mention a few.

Recently Diego has achieved the certification of Pro Tools post production operator.

Coming from a Rock/Metal background, throughout the years he has developed a vast experience in playing a wealth of different musical genres, putting a strong focus on Blues/Funk/Soul music. Recently he has diversified to accompany singers in acoustic settings.

Currently Diego is teaching guitar and playing contemporary music along side numerous international artists.

Rafael Lopes is a UK based saxophonist that has a vast range of professional experiences as a musician performing for fifteen years. Since moving to the UK in 2013 he’s been an active member of both London and Birmingham music scene, working as a session musician doing diverse work from studio recordings, functions, events, theatre shows, cabarets, tribute bands and most recently improvising alongside Dj’s or backing tracks.

He has performed alongside some of the most prestigious UK and Europe’s artists and some of his most relevant performances includes playing alongside 12 times Grammy-nominated artist and holder of one Grammy for “Best Gospel Song” in 2011 saxophonist Kirk Whalum which became famous for his feature on Witney Houston’s track “I Will Always Love You”. 

Since his start in the Dj & sax scene in 2014 covering for UK/Birmingham based saxophonist Andy Sax and having graduated from the Birmingham Conservatoire with a degree in jazz, Rafael Sax has acquired and developed an unique style, reputation and overall trust from his clients, which makes him a very reliable and strong candidate when considering a saxophonist to play with DJ’s improvising alongside all genres of music such as funk, jazz, hip-hop, R&B, Latin, disco, house, 80’s and chillout music. 

Finally Rafael sax has performed on the DJ & sax scene for over a decade on some of the most prestigious venues nationally and internationally such as Birmingham’s Fumo, Nuvo Bar, Chameleon, Players, Dirty Martini, Sports & Music bar in Liverpool, The Warehouse in Leeds, Charlie Wright’s Jazz Club in London, Mostly Jazz & Blues Festival and also overseas in the town of Amora (Portugal) where he was resident sax player for two consecutive summer seasons playing at Ibiza style parties alongside different Djs, electric violin, percussionists and vocals.

My name is Zachary David Hill (nickname/stage name: Zach~Attack) and I am a vocalist with experience in International Top 40 bands (party bands) performing at 5 star hotels/resorts in the Middle East and Asia, musical theater as well as a lounge singer providing entertainment at local taverns/restaurants (with all of my own equipment). I sing a wide variety of repertoire including everything from Top 40's, Classic Rock, Hard Rock, Rock, Blues, Soul, Country, R&B etc.

Having been described as a natural entertainer with a passion for singing and performing, Zachary David Hill’s powerful and warm voice has entertained audiences all over South Africa as well as in the United Arab Emirates (UAE), Kingdom of Bahrain and in Chengdu, China. Zachary (or Zach-Attack as his nickname/stage name) always fostered a love for singing but found his voice in High School after joining the choir and thereafter deciding to take his singing to a more serious level with seven consecutive years of contemporary vocal training with the same vocal coach. Before Graduating from High school, he was named Student of the Year (the highest honour) at his voice training academy in 2008; after being named Head of his High School Choir his first major competition win was The Dockyard Theatre’s competition ‘So You Think You Can Sing.’ This was only the beginning as many gigs soon followed with performances at restaurants, taverns, casinos as well as on stages all around the country. He went on to complete his Undergraduate Bachelor of Arts Degree majoring in Psychology and English and whilst studying in his first year out of school he fronted a Hard Rock Band called H.E.R.O. for approximately a year. Thereafter he continued to perform whilst studying taking part in various shows such as ‘Vintage Rocks’, ‘Sting, Adams & U2’ (Tribute) and ‘The King, The Boss & the Great Pretender.’ He took part in the South African Talent Championships in August 2010 in which he won four Gold and two Gold Plus medals and was thereafter selected as one of forty individuals to represent South Africa at the Bi-Annual talent convention AMTC held in Orlando, Florida, USA in January 2011. It was here that he competed at an international level before talented peers, observers, agents, managers, industry professionals and casting directors having excelled in the performing arts. Upon his return, Zachary won other singing competitions as time passed such as the Cottonfields Idols competition in 2011 as well as the Locals with Vocals competition in 2012. He had a resident gig at the African Peninsula restaurant close to his home where he performed every Friday night for about 2 years from January 2011 to January 2013. 
After finishing the first of his two-year Honours Degree in Psychology(2012), all this experience eventually led to the successful selection as one of the lead singers in the Barnyard Theatre’s (South Africa’s premier Theatre group) production of ‘Thank You For The Music’-Tribute to Queen, Bee Gees & Abba. He toured South Africa with the show in 2013 and 2014 whilst simultaneously completing his Honours Degree in Psychology. With the culmination of the Barnyard Theatre show in July 2014, Zachary successfully auditioned and was chosen by El-Live Productions as the lead male singer to front a five piece band, called BOLT. This resulted in a yearlong contract in which the band performed at a venue inside the hotel five nights a week at the Hilton, Al Ain, UAE. Zachary was then selected to join the band UNITY as a vocalist for a new upcoming contract at Music House in Chengdu, China through Blast Productions. They performed there six nights a week for two months (November 3 2015-January 3 2016) and thereafter traveled back to the Emirates for their next contract at the Jazz Bar at the Hilton Abu Dhabi Corniche. Here Unity performed 6 nights a week on that contract for 5 months until June 2016. The rest of the year consisted of completing a TEFL Certification (Teaching English as a Foreign Language) and freelance gigs whilst simultaneously working to secure the next gig abroad whether it be Hotels or Cruise Ships.
Most recently Zachary was recruited to join a band to replace a male vocalist at Davy Crockett’s at the Diva Hotel in Manama, Bahrain, where he performed for 3 months (February-May 2017) finishing the contract with them until Ramadan. 
Possessing a strong and captivating voice, a confident stage presence and wide knowledge of music, Zachary is a dynamic singer who is always determined to bring all his passion and raw energy into every one of his performances.

column38:
Will Spurling began playing the drums at the age of eight, after his parents finally agreed to buy him a drum kit as they had endured years of him beat boxing and drumming on their pots and pans.
He began his professional career playing drums for a variety of Indie bands around the toilet circuit of London and also toured the UK.  He was once described by Indie Music fanzine Drowned In Sound as “The finest drummer on the scene”.  He also gained a reputation for dropping his trousers at the end of a gig and dry humping the drum kit - the audience loved it, always, BELIEVE!
Since then he has become a much sought after drummer for all kinds of events, including weddings, pubs/clubs and corporate events.  This is because he is as comfortable playing a swinging ballad as he is playing heavy rock or drum&bass.  The spirit of jazz is a part of him, always listening to what is going around him musically and improvising in the moment.

I am a professional drummer based in London, UK and have by now performed with a variety of acts, from philharmonic and symphonic bands, to function, tribute and original bands and artists.

Throughout the last 15 years, I have gathered extensive experience both in the studio and performing live in a variety of music genres and styles, which spread from classical and folk to pop, blues, soul, rock, jazz and metal music, among others. 

​Some of the names I've played drums (and sometimes BV's) for include Anthony James, Elliott Millman, Izzie Naylor, Abi Farrell, I Talk To Strangers, RESET Function Band, What The Floyd, among others. I was also a key percussionist in the Philharmonic of Arrabal as well as in the Leiria Symphonic Band.

I have performed countless function gigs back in Portugal, and in the UK, not just on the drums, but also on acoustic guitar and BV's. I also work with the agency Earcandy as a Performer (Drums) for their Indie act, 'The Indie Heads'.

Between these, I offer remote a drum recording service, I write and produce my own music, and also teach drums.

column38:
You may have seen her perform on BBC Television or at The
Royal Shakespeare Company, but did you know TALIA has been
capturing the hearts of her audience since she was seven? Her
passion for music transcends language and genres and was
ignited when her father took her to an accordion shop to
choose her own. Before that, she spent much of her childhood
singing with her father while he played the concertina, a
Northern Portugal folk instrument. She loved singing and grew
to fully understand the pure joy of giving music to others.
TALIA is multi-talented and is always looking to improve the
lives of others, be it through her moving and lively traditional
music, her workshops, or original songs, such as “Happy Go London.” After spending time in the studio recording her First Ever Album that was achieved through her Crowdfunding Project, she now intends to increase her original pieces as she progresses through her rich musical life. TALIA plays English and American 80’s and 90’s pop music with more recent numbers. She sings in English with a twist of Portuguese and Spanish and is equally happy playing both at intimate events and on a grand scale.

column38:
I'm a freelance singer/musician based in London.

ANSWERING YOUR QUESTIONS:
Your artist name: Luke Tanner
Social media links: IG @luketanner_uk 
Links to music: https://youtu.be/rVhEWOL702Q - Live Showreel 2021

MY SETLIST:
Depending on your needs, I can tailor setlists based on your requirements. I usually perform some 'pub classics' together with chart hits to please and entertain the crowd. But if you'd like something more settle, I can perform some laid-back 'background' music too. I specialize in rock/country music usually.
I have wide experience in performing in pubs as a solo, and I can offer a one-man-band solo with a loop pedal, stomp box, and tambourine if you'd like.

I own my own PA system, therefore I'm completely independent and I can provide any necessary gear to suit your needs.
Please find attached my EPK for more information. 
-------------------------------
ABOUT ME:
I have a wide experience on stage and studio, and I regularly street perform in Westminster, in locations such as Covent Garden, Trafalgar Square, and Leicester Square. I'm at ease in entertaining large crowds, make them dance, and interact with my set while delivering the best quality performance possible.

I started singing at the age of 14 years old, studying 'Bel Canto' to then move on to a more modern approach based on Pop and Rock styles. I've been singing for 15 years now, performed on various venues as part of bands or one-man show gigs.
I can play guitar, bass and have a wide vocal tenor range which allows me to perform most styles and songs.

Looking forward to hearing from you

Kind regards,
Luke Tanner

Kaspar grew up in London and moved to Hertfordshire when he was 9. He learned the guitar when he was 11 when a family friend with a serious disability gave one to him on the condition that Kaspar would learn how to play it. 

At the age of 12 he discovered Pride and Joy on Guitar Hero and quickly realised this was the blues. This would send him on a path of education and revelation to the blues as a genre and a life style. 

 Kaspar went to New Orleans to celebrate turning 18 where he performed in B.B. Kings Blues Club (and was discovered by Harold Marcho music producer). Harold invited Kaspar back to the states to record his first single Sailing.

He is now performing with his live band, Kaspar & The Swamp Dogs around St Albans, London and where ever he may be invited to. The band consists of Seb Rejal on bass guitar and drummer Mia Casey.

Gustavo Roriz is a string instrumentalist that takes us from Portugal to Brazil, Latin America, Africa, Mediterranean Europe and beyond, on a journey of sounds, rhythms and colours.
As far as his style and influences, you can find nuances of African and Brazilian music, jazz, fado, and much more. He is also a true entertainer who loves to tell stories, sip wine and chat late into the night. His career has focused on highlighting the links between Portugal and Brazil, bringing their sound identities and his musical personality to the four corners of the world.
He has performed at renown venues such as Coliseu in Lisbon, Porto and Buenos Aires, Sala Zitarrosa in Montevideo, Olga Cadaval Cultural Center, Teatro Calderon in Madrid, Carnegie Hall in New York, Bauhaus Music in Amsterdam, and many others.
His career started in 1996, and he has achieved significant praise and recognition in his field. He has participated in many recordings and released two albums, one solo and one with D’alambre.
He’s a Music graduate from UNICAMP (Brazil) and has a Master’s degree in Jazz Performance from ESML (Superior School of Music – Lisbon).

Tara Cunningham is an instrumentalist and composer based in London. Her work as a band member, collaborator and improviser has been described as having a "distinctive style that draws on both jazz and avant-rock... genuinely impressive" (Ian Mann ").
 
Tara grew up in Bath, and took an interest in music from an early age, heavily influenced by her father's record collection consisting mainly of 70s and 80s avant-pop. She started to play the guitar aged 8 and from there played in multiple bands with her peers. At 16 years old she began to attend the junior jazz course at the Royal Academy of Music before moving to London at 18 to study a BMus in Jazz guitar at Trinity Laban Conservatoire of Music. It was here that she studied improvisation with Hans Koller, Lauren Kinsella, Liam Noble and Asaf Sirkis among many others.
 
Now making her mark on the UK music scene, Tara is a collaborator in multiple projects including industrial jazz 5-piece 'The Private View', jazz-folk trio 'Giant Folk', and dream-pop singer Natalie Wildgoose. With these and many more she regularly performs in London's most celebrated venues, collecting praise from audiences, peers and critics alike, with a versatile and evolving skillset, but first and foremost "a wonderful guitarist" (Steve Mead - Manchester Jazz Festival).

column38:
Felipe Drago
dragodrumsstudio@gmail.com
07891 643 137
www.felipedrago.com

Graduated by the Musicians Institute (MI), home of the Percussion Institute of Technology (PIT), in Los Angeles, California, in the Professional Percussion Player Program, March/1996. 
Drummer since 1984, becoming professional in 1989. Since 1986 participates actively in musical groups, has great experience on stage, studio and teaching. Took part in many tours in Brazil some regions of Argentina and south of the USA. 
In 1992 started his activities as a drum kit teacher. 
In 1995 relocated to the USA with the purpose of studying in the above-cited school.  After graduating at PIT worked playing with artists, in recording sessions and as a teacher at the Eubanks Conservatory of Music and Arts, where in September of 1996, became the Percussion Department Director. 
Coming back to Brazil, in 1998, Felipe started to develop his own teaching method, using his experience as student at MI and private lessons, and as a teacher. He has written methods for absolute beginners, for Rock Drumming, Rudiments, Playing Techniques and Rhythmic Reading, all methods use Accelerative Learning Techniques. These methods were used at COM (Centro de Orientacao Musical), Sao Paulo, Brazil, during the years of 2001-2002.
After teaching privately and playing as a session musician and side-man, in 2003 became the Drums and Percussion Department Director at Voice Musical Centre (www.voice.art.br), also in Sao Paulo, Brazil.
From 2004 until 2012 Felipe was a very active collaborator of the magazine Modern Drummer Brasil, 
In 2006 Felipe decided to relocate to London in search of new horizons, new challenges and new musical opportunities. Since then Felipe has been teaching, doing session work and live performances.
From 2007 until 2012 was part of the teaching team of The Planet Drum (www.theplanetdrum.co.uk) when after this 5-year stint decided to open his own teaching practice.
 Since 2011 Felipe is an active member of a new rock outfit in London, Cherry White (www.cherrywhitemusic.com) who are being considered the new bearers of the classic rock banner, having recorded one EP, one CD and have just released a new single which is being very well received by the specialised press.
2014 has brought a lot of good news. Since October 2013 Felipe has been taking drums lessons again with drummer extraordinaire, drums ambassador and educator Dom Famularo (www.domfamularo.com) from New York, the lessons are via Skype. Dom has recognised in Felipe the competence, the capacity and the passion making him part of his selected team of educators and also referring him to the education teams of D’Addario and Sabian (two major drum manufacturing companies who are serious about drumming education.). Felipe is a member of the D’Addario Education Collective and Sabian Education Network and is a proud endorser of Promark drumsticks and Evans drumheads.
In March 2014 Felipe went to Frankfurt for the Musikmesse, an international fair for music instruments and all articles related to music, invited by Dom Famularo and the above-cited companies. In the same weekend Felipe went to Paris for the Vic Firth’s Private Drums Teachers Seminar, led by Mr Famularo and with the presence of the legendary Vic Firth himself.
From 2nd to 6th June Felipe has spend a whole week at WizDom Drumshed in Long Island, NY with the great Dom Famularo for a period of coaching. One-to-one lessons from 9am to 6pm.
In 2016 Felipe had his first book published, Play the Drums by Wizdom Media, a book for beginners using Accelerated Learning techniques, making the process of learning the instrument faster and more fun.
July 2018 brought one of the greatest experiences in Felipe’s career. He received a scholarship from Sabian Education Network to attend Billy Cobham’s Art of the Rhythm Section Retreat, in Mesa, Arizona. He spent a whole week having lessons with Billy Cobham and his band about the art of playing in a rhythm section.
On 30th September 2018 Felipe was part of the Sabian Education Network panel at the UK Drum Show in Manchester.


Felipe Drago

Born in Brazil, Felipe Drago began playing the drums in 1984. He later moved to Los Angeles to study music and graduated from the Musicians’ Institute (MI) in 1996. He has worked extensively as a recording artist and performer in both L.A. and in Brazil. As his career grew, so did his reputation for not only playing the drums, but for teaching the drums too.

Drago has worked extensively as an extremely active contributor to music manufacturing industry websites and programmes including Modern Drummer Brasil and more recently, Drum Education Live. His programmes and methodologies have been adopted by COM (Centro de Orientacao Musical), and Voice Musical Centre of Sao Paulo, Brazil, as well as the Eubanks Conservatory of Music and Arts and Planet Drum.

Now residing in London, Drago has studied with famed drummers, Dom Famularo and Billy Cobham and is now a respected member of the Sabian Education Network (SEN) and D’Addario Education Collective. 

In more recent years, Drago’s accomplishments include the publication of his first book, Play the Drums (Wizdom Media/Alfred 2016) and speaking on the Sabian Education Network panel of experts at the 2018 UK Drum Show.

A regular attendee of drum and percussion events as far afield as Paris, Arizona and California, Felipe has become a prominent figure in the drum manufacturing industry who continues to run a successful teaching practice in London. He is a proud endorser of Evans and Promark.

General Musical Background
Pete Murray studied guitar and music theory at the London Guitar Institute. Pete has
played many styles of music from Rock to Jazz including Pop, Blues, country and
World Music with some great musicians, including Pete Riley (Keith Emerson),
Geraint Roberts, Bill Worrall (Barbara Thompson) , Robert Rickenberg (Sheena
Davies), Joe Hubbard (Ben E. King, Morrissy Mullen), Kenrick Rowe (Jazz
Jamaica), Rod Young (Natalie Cole, Hugh masekela) and Derek Nash (Jools Holland
Band). He has also played on, arranged and produced for records such as World
music artist Dalinda’s Waternixie. Pete has played at many venues including The
Euro Woodstock Festival, Hungary, along side bands Jethro Tull, Jetherson Air plane
and The Byrds.
Function Work Experience
Pete is also very experienced in the function field working for different function
bands that cover a wide range of musical styles. He started his own jazz band in 1994
playing extensively around Great Britain including a residency at the Royal Lancaster
Hotel, Hyde Park that year. This continued with functions at such places as
Claridges, The Park Lane Hotel, The International Conference Centre, Pinewood
studios, Kensington Roof Gardens, The B.T. Tower, Madejski Stadium, The
Hurlingham Club, Greenwich Dome, One White Hall Place, Kew Gardens, and
Highclere Castle for clients such as Lady Stevens (her Wedding at 30 pavilion Road)
Henley Royal Regatta, Wentworth Golf Tournament, the BBC and celebrities such as
Sir Terry Wogan.

Michael is a professional bass player, multi-instrumentalist and songwriter based in Guildford, Surrey.

Since graduating with a degree in Music Performance from ACM in Guildford, Michael has developed as an MD and exceptional live performer and has played over 300 events for a variety function bands and agencies. column38:
I have been singing for the last 15 years, and ever since I joined a local amateur theatre group at the age of 16, I just knew singing would be a part of my life for a long time coming. 
I studied music at college, and went on to train professionally at Colchester Institute, and further at Leicester College of Performing Arts gaining qualifications in both classical and theatre singing. After sustaining numerous injuries during dance training, I decided to come out of professional training and pursue singing on my own. 
I since began a career in the fitness industry, working in Spa and Leisure Management, and continued to sing just because it made me happy, and that's the most fulfilling part.
And now, I am on this journey of performing because I absolutely love the joy it brings other people. Whether it's performing in a bar enhancing the ambience of the evening, or performing at a birthday, wedding or anniversary party and feeling the happiness emulating from people when they sing along to the classics, I love every second of it.

column38:
United Kingdom based Leonardo Giuliani, is an independent musician, guitar player and singer-songwriter, whose music revolves around and is influenced by Rock, Blues, Country and Folk. 

Working and dedicating his life to music and to keeping it organic, honest, and meaningful. Striving to make others feel like music makes him feel.
In 2019 he had the honour to be invited to sit in with Buddy Guy at his Legends club in Chicago, one of his Blues influences and a guitar icon.

His debut album is currently in post production and scheduled to be released later this year.
The record is produced by four-time Grammy award winner producer Tom Hambridge, who also plays drums on the album, and presents Phil Palmer on rhythm guitar and Robben Ford as a guest on one song.

His sound is primarily influenced by those artists and bands from the late ’60 and 70’s, from acoustic singer-songwriters to electric jam bands, all of whom played something different every night and made every performance unique.

Violeta Skya has proven yet again that not many artists have the persistence, drive, longevity and sheer sizzle that she does! Multi-lingual, with a diverse cultural background, the artist, born in Vilnius, Lithuania to Slavic parents, grew up surrounded by many different types of music. As a teenager, after years of music education, and training as a classical pianist, Violeta decided to become a full time musician and went to study pop and jazz vocals at Vilnius Conservatory. Many performance opportunities came along. At that time she performed a lot in various festivals and city concerts and started work as a session singer. Performing for large audiences at international contests in Europe, (winning several in Sweden, Spain and Latvia) enabled her to develop as an artist and stage performer. Concerts, jazz gigs and numerous function bookings have been her playground for an entire life.

During her vocal journey she has successfully sung a wide range of musical styles from jazz to rock. Moving to London in 2014 to take her musical development to the next level. Recently she finished her second degree on the BMus at BIMM London. The new life in the UK helped to channel her artistic expression and Violeta has found her niche and original sound, uniquely combining pop with r'n'b and dance music, creating a sexy, smart and glamorous vibe. Violeta is known for her talents as a songwriter, topliner and memorable 'hook' maker. As a lyricist she emphasises the power of feeling. A story behind lyrics is important for her and she puts a new message in every song. Emotional, often with a dark and edgy vibe, her songs and her versatile vocals leave audiences in a jaw-dropped state. They frequently introduce her music to their friends and encourage other followers to engage with her career.

Violeta Skya released her debut single 'Kiss Me' with the sensual and glamorous video to support the music. The song was noticed almost instantly and is played on eight radio stations in different countries including the USA, UK and Italy following with many radio interviews and features on different music blogs worldwide.

At the moment Violeta is recording a lot of new original material and is planning new music videos coming soon. Her collaborations with established music producers, several of whom also work for major record labels, have enabled her to network with management companies, which are now interested in her music.

column38:
In 2014 Glenn was awarded a scholarship to study at the Elder Conservatorium, Adelaide, Australia (2015-2017)  While there he studied under John McDermott, Derek Pascoe, James Muller, Dusty Cox and Mark Fergusson. In his first year at uni Glenn started playing for the Derek Pascoe Quartet, Donnarumma and Time No Changes (trio), as well as depping on various function and big band shows. Glenn backed jazz violinist, Julian Ferarretto and the Adelaide University Orchestra at the Elder Hall as part of the 2017 Australian Composer’s Showcase. Also with the Latin Ensemble, Glenn has recorded two albums (Downbeat award nominees) and toured South Australia and Victoria. Since moving back to the UK at the end of 2017, Glenn continued his study under Elliott Henshaw and Ralph Salmins (2018) and most recently become a student of Peter Erskine (2020-onwards). 
Glenn is currently on tour with the Bat out of Hell UK tour 2021/22 as drum deputy and has recently toured with and played for The Ultimate Buble Tour, Jersey Boys, The John Wheatcroft Electric Band, the Glenn Charles Quintet. 
Glenn records remotely from his home in the midlands where he has recently been working with Tyler Venter on various projects, William Markham, Luke Stevenson on his EP; tracking for Iplaybass for Film and TV; and with Jack Smith on his musical variety album. 
Glenn is currently writing and producing 4 contrasting jazz/fusion, Big Band and Latin projects that will be released in the UK and Australia.

column38:
Born in Chile, Gigliola began playing guitar at a young age, inspired by seeing her father play guitar and music records whenever he was at home. Having learned the basics from her father, she joined the folklore group at her school and performed with them regularly.
She carried on teaching herself how to play by listening to her favourite music and once she moved to Italy with her family in her teenage years, she started playing and performing with classic rock bands. 

After graduating from her school in Italy, Gigliola wanted to take her passion and talent for music to a professional level. She moved to England in 2010 to study at The Academy of Contemporary Music (ACM) in Guildford where she studied advanced guitar techniques in different genres and styles, performance skills, songwriting, music theory and music business. She had the honor of studying under great guitarists such as  Michael Casswell, Pete Friesen, Pete Roth and Carl Orr who have worked and performed with some of the finest musicians in the world.

During and after her time at the ACM, she joined and formed original bands to develop her passion for writing music as well as teaching guitar to private students and in schools.

Gigliola performs regularly at corporate events, weddings, festivals and pubs, providing professional entertainment with her function and original bands.

I have been a professional bass player, multi-instrumentalist (vocals, guitar, keyboards, saxophone, to varying levels) and producer for the last 15 years. I have a degree in popular music from Goldsmiths uni in London and have toured all over Europe and North America with multiple bands as well as countless UK-wide gigs with covers bands and collectives.
I'm based just outside Ely, Cambridgeshire and I'm 33 years old.

Since 2014 I have been doing function work for several collective-type bands and a local fixed line-up band in which I play bass and keyboards often at the same time (see promo videos) on a freelance basis and I also often handle the audio mixing duties for this as well.

I also currently play bass with the multi-million album selling metal band Annihilator (since 2015) with whom I've played regularly to crowds ranging from 1,000 to 60,000 in size, although this touring is fairly sparse and gives me lots of time for other gigging.
I have recorded bass on hundreds of commercially released albums for a variety of other artists and my own bands, many reaching the top of rock album charts.

Although my background is primarily in rock and metal (I realise the latter is quite niche!), I have a lot of experience in many other styles such as funk, motown, pop and in between.

I have my own transport and plenty of professional audio gear including being endorsed by Dingwall basses & Roland and use Fractal Audio, Carvin and Taurus amplification. I am also experienced with both in ear monitors (I have my own wireless systems) and wedges.
column41:
I also play keyboards live professionally although I would not consider myself an expert, a lot of my keyboard playing is at the same time as playing bass (see promo videos). I also have a reasonably high level of rock guitar, although bass in my main and most versatile instrument.
I am a fast and enthusiastic learner of new material whenever needed, always wanting to sound my best and help any band I'm playing with to be better. I provide vocal arrangements and create backing tracks for many bands wherever applicable. I am more than happy to travel any distance for the right gig. I also have a full Roland TD-20 electric drum kit that I currently rent out which can be available for gigs.
Thank you and I look forward to hearing from you.

column38: Stephen Ellery plays alto and soprano sax. He has played jazz, funk, reggae, rock, blues and pop. He also arranges for orchestras and conducts orchestras. He works as a guest conductor in Europe, Latin America and Japan. In London and around he plays Brazilian music and soul and funk with various bands.

For James, music has been at the centre of his world since a young age. An excellent performer, he has breadth and depth to his talent; achieving grade VIIIs on the piano, cello and clarinet, along with grades on percussion. His early musical ability was recognised with scholarships to Salisbury Cathedral and Sherborne School, where he studied under Jaime Henderson. Winner of the Haliday (2011, 2015) and Patrick Shelley (2014) music competitions, he was awarded the prize for the Music School’s Contribution to Music at Commemoration on leaving the school in 2015.
In 2016 he progressed to Leeds College of Music, where he graduated with a First Class Degree in 2019. He gained a ARSM piano performance diploma in 2017.
James’s fluent and highly confident style is not limited to any one genre; he played keyboard in Sherborne Swing Band, touring Barbados in 2014 with swing, be bop and blues numbers. Classical skills were furthered while studying under Jeremy Davis in London and Svetlana Pinegina in Leeds. He is as much at ease with Mussorgsky, as with Duke Ellington or Adele.
He has recently completed a contract performing aboard the Spirit of Adventure as part of the in house band. Matinee and nightly performances involved cast shows with the Spirit Show Company and backing guest cabaret acts.
Other notable appearances include a performance for HM Queen Elizabeth and Duke of Edinburgh in Sherborne Abbey on their Golden Jubilee Your of 2012, playing piano for a One Act Play in Middle Temple, London (2015), Jazz repertoire for a drinks reception in Berkely Square, Mayfair (2016), and various hospitality locations on the South Coast.

I have a huge passion for playing the drums and a wealth of experience within the music industry that has been built up throughout my time performing live and in the studio with a multitude of different bands and artists covering a very wide spectrum of musical styles. I live and breathe drums!

I was part of a band called 'Lisp' with whom I recorded and relased my first album with whilst signed to London Records. We also played live extensively throughout the UK and toured on a number of occasions with Turin Brakes, Rodney P and Max Tundra.

I still perform and record with other artists on a freelance basis and gig regularly with The Bootleg Bee Gees and a Bruce Springsteen Tribute Band (See promo video).
I also perform very regularly in several function bands, one called Meantime which includes Phil Veacock (Sax and musical director) and Jon Scott (trumpet) who are part of Jools Holland's Rhythm And Blues Orchestra. Others are The Serious Hat Band and The Top Hat Band both part of Dewson Entertainments (www.hatband.co.uk) who are both amongst some of the busiest bands in the country.

Additionally I have gigged in Owen Paul’s band who enjoyed success with the no.3 hit “My Favourite Waste Of Time”. I too have toured with Theatre productions in the UK and Europe where I have worked closely with musical directors.

I teach the drums privately and have gained and built up many regular private students. I've been playing drums for over 30 years now and teaching drums for the past 20 years.
Many people have kindly referred to me as "The Clock" due to my timekeeping skills behind the kit and a few of my drum heroes/influences are John Bonham, Keith Moon, Buddy Rich, Stewart Copeland, Chad Smith, Steve Gadd, Vinnie Colaiuta and too many others to mention.

column38: Allison Mareek is a multicultural singer and songwriter. Now based in London, where she has found a true melting pot that mirrors her own roots, she’s weaving these influences into a sound that is both familiar and unique. A sound where soulful melodies meet jazz standards, ear-catching blue notes, and a minimalist pop production - blending as many different colors and routes as the ones that have dotted her storyline so far.

column38:
Sar/Lou is a 37-year-old female bassist from Staffordshire in the UK. Having first picked up a bass when she was 13, she has spent her years honing her craft and skills, including gigging with bands around local pub circuits.

While her primary genres include rock, pop and metal, she likes to play all styles. Her main influences include Tim Commerford (Rage Against the Machine), Duff McKagan (Guns n Roses), Chris Wolstenholme (Muse), Eric Friedman/Brian Marshall (Alter Bridge/Tremonti), Divinity Roxx (Beyonce), Gail Ann Dorsey (Bowie, Prince) and Geddy Lee (Rush).

In 2021, she started studying a BA Hons in Music Industry Practice with Bass Guitar specialism at the world-renowned Academy of Contemporary Music (ACM) in Birmingham, setting her sights on taking her musicianship to the next level and becoming a professional session musician. At ACM, Sar/Lou is also the Student Ambassador and Student Council Rep for Birmingham Campus.

She is an endorsed Ashdown Artist, and currently working on original music with bands Resting B!tch Face and VSHA.

Sar/Lou currently offers private tuition for all ages both online and in person, whatever your goal. She is registered with RSL Awards and as an Associate of the Registry of Guitar tutors, and so able to offer lessons based around the Rockschool and RGT syllabuses for those wishing to work towards graded exams.

Tech spec, PAT test certificates, DBS, Public Liability/Indemnity Insurance and Musicians Union Membership details available upon request.

Emma Saville is an evocative British singer/songwriter based between London & Sheffield, UK, with a combined following of over 50k. She's performed across the UK, & in Brazil, Costa Rica, Colombia, Cuba, Spain, Australia & New Zealand. Combining emotive lyrics, memorable storytelling & "breathtakingly-beautiful vocals”, Emma creates music that “moves the head, heart & feet”. Emma's multi-tonal voice has been described as “powerful with an impressive range…will give you goosebumps", & compared to Sade, Anita Baker & Freya Riding. Her songs have been streamed millions of times in 143 countries, viewed over 260k times on YouTube & featured multiple times on BBC Radio. Emma’s sultry music is a rich blend of soul, hiphop & R&B, with Latin influences. Her distinctive voice is still finding new fans.

Follow @EmmaSavilleMusic on all socials or visit EmmaSavilleMusic.com. 
Contact EmmaSavilleMusic@gmail.com for press, collaborations or bookings.

Rikard is a freelance bass guitarist, guitarist and teacher currently based in North London. He is a member of the Musicians' Union (MU) and has an Enhanced DBS Check.


Hailing from Sweden, Rikard took the life changing decision to move to London to pursue a career in music. He enrolled at Tech Music school (now BIMM London) in 2013, under the tutelage of professional bass players such as Andrew McKinney (James Taylor Quartet), Rob Statham (Mike Stern, Jim Mullen, Keith Emerson), Dave Troke (The Overtones, The Noisettes, Donna Summer) and Svetlana Vassileva (The Vegastones, Glenn Tilbrook, Moby). During this time, he also performed in masterclasses with artists such as Peredur ap Gwynedd (Pendulum), and Ed Soph (Bill Evans, Glenn Miller Orchestra).


Since graduating in 2016, Rikard has worked in musical theatre, played with function bands and with original bands/artists, and played at festivals both in Sweden and in England. His playing has taken him to Italy, Portugal, Czech Republic, Cyprus, Oman, The Maldives, to name but a few!


Since 2015, Rikard has been keeping the low end pumping in Fully Funktional - a top UK function band who play throughout the UK and internationally for high-profile exclusive events and venues such as Royal Ascot, Grosvenor House, Wembley Stadium, Chandlers Cross and Sheraton hotel.

Hailing from Camden Town in Central London, Christivie Francisco, better known as Christavie, is an up and coming artist with a penchant for writing infectious music and relatable narratives. Inspired greatly by internationally acclaimed artists like Adele and Michael Jackson, Christavie’s music journey began at a tender age where he started listening to and performing covers in school plays. Christavie participated in many choirs and was even appointed the lead singer of a regional Jazz band/ When he discovered the magic of music and the joy it can bring to people, everything changed as it sparked a burning passion for him to pursue music later on in life.
 
Having put out “Risking It All”, an 80s inspired pop song as well as an EP titled “Front Door”, Christavie’s unique sound has been bubbling under the surface for a while now. Although promotion and performance opportunities were limited during the launch of his EP due to the pandemic, Christavie still pushed through and is slowly amassing a substantial group of fans. The artist hopes to continue to break the boundaries of creativity and is gearing up to release his upcoming EP “Hypotic” which consist of music made solely on the iPad’s version of Garageband. Christavie is also preparing to produce his very first professionally recorded EP which is slated to drop in Spring 2022.
 
Christavie is no doubt, well on his way to becoming one of the most phenomenal music Artists in this generation.

Laura has established herself as a prominent freelance harpist in London, appearing in a multitude of orchestral, chamber music and solo performances in the region. She has performed at such venues as Cadogan Hall, Sadler’s Wells Theatre, and Teatro Real in Madrid where she collaborated with Sinfónica Verum and ballet Víctor Ullate. She began her professional studies at Conservatorio Superior de Música de Aragón. She then moved to London to complete a second degree at Trinity Laban Conservatoire of Music and Dance with Gabriella dall’Olio and Frances Kelly, where she also completed a Master’s Degree. During this time she developed an additional interest in creating electronic music from electroacoustic harp and sound processing.

Laura has a keen interest in contemporary performance practices and interdisciplinary collaborations. She has appeared with artist such as Chiara Dubey and Livia Rita, and has written her own arrangements of popular music for the electric harp with live looping.

Laura maintains an active schedule of event performing, playing at weddings, funerals, receptions, and corporate events. She is a recurring musician at the Chelsea Old Town Hall, Cadogan Hotel and the Sofitel St-James. As well as being a notable freelance performer, Laura maintains a full-time teaching studio for pupils of all ages and abilities.

column38:
Emanuele “EMC” Marchetti is an Italian naturalized British freelance session drummer and drum teacher. Born and raised in Ancona, Italy, he grew up in a musical family; his father (classical ﬂute player and music teacher) pushed him, at a very young age, into classical studies choosing violin as an instrument that would have developed his perfect pitch whereas his mother (a self-taught singer) shared with him the passion for music by playing many different records in the house. He started studying violin and piano at “Conservatorio di Musica G.B. Pergolesi” where he accomplished a Diploma in Music Theory and Solfeggio in 1997. After eleven and a half years of classical studies, due to his loss of interest for violin and the lack of motivation from his teachers, he decided to quit music and discover a new world, the Hip Hop Culture.

Through Hip Hop and its lifestyle, he fell in love with B-Boying (aka breakdance) which is gonna bring him to travel around the Europe and competing in several national and international dance contests; at the same time, it also made contact with another discipline, the DJing; the two combined together allowed him to understand the deep connection between the dance and the music, also the passion for vinyls collection and the research of the sound for the dancers have opened up the doors to all the 70’s and 80’s Funk, Jazz-Funk, Jazz-Rock, Fusion as well as 90’s golden age Rap Music. The wide listening of Break Beats and Grooves pushed him to resume his musical studies and ﬁnally land to his instrument: the drums. After studying for a year as a self-taught, it decided to push the approach to drums to a further and deeper level by taking private lessons with Andrea Morandi who gave him a huge musical and technical background.

He studied for a couple of years at LAJ “Libera Accademia del Jazz” under the guidance of Andrea Morandi and Roberto Gazzani, performing at “Camerino Jazz Festival” and “Fano Jazz by the sea”. In 2015, motivated by his dream to pursue a career in music, he decided to relocate to London. In 2016, he accomplished a Higher Diploma in Music Performance at ICMP “The Institute of Contemporary Music and Performance”. In 2019, he accomplished a First Class Honors Degree in Creative Music Performance at DIME Online “Detroit Institute of Music Education” associated with the Falmouth University in UK. He studied privately with Gabor Dornyei (Thunder Duo, Stanley Jordan), Chuck Sabo (Elton John, Natalie Imbruglia, Marcella Detroit), Fergus Gerrand (Sting, Duran Duran, Take That, Spice Girls, Morcheeba, Whitesnake, Brian Adams), Mike Sturgis (Wishbone Ash, Elton John, A-Ha, 21 Guns, Bob James, Natalie Cole), Paul Elliot and, recently, with the legendary funk master David Garibaldi (Tower of Power).

He also attended several masterclasses and clinics with master drummers like Benny Greb, Christian Meyer, Josh Dion, Mark Guiliana, Steve Smith and Horacio “El Negro” Hernandez. London based, he’s keeping a very busy schedule between performing live and teaching: as a performer he played some of the biggest festivals in the UK such as Glastonbury, Isle of Wight, Jazz & Blues Callander Festival, and Upton Blues Festival. In 2020 he performed live in a TV Show for Manoto TV at Wimbledon Studios playing for the well known Iranian singer Mansour.

In March 2021 he’s getting featured in Rhythm Magazine in the article written
by Mike Sturgis entitled “Positivity and Resilience in a global pandemic”
where he talks about his experience of staying positive and creative despite
the total loss of work caused by the pandemic and reinventing himself with
new skills acquired during the lockdown.

As a teacher, he’s the drum tutor of the London Music School in Hackney Wick and the Pinner Drum School in North West London directed by Gabor Dorniey; he’s also giving 1-to-1 private in-person and online lessons. His approach to teaching is not only related to passing on the knowledge of
drumming and music, but it goes further and deeper into helping, empowering, and motivating people to discover their true potential through art and music.

Credits: “Emanuele is one of the most dedicated and focused young players on the scene today. His talent and super hard work will 100% ensure that we’ll hear about him and his achievements in the music business a lot very soon!” GABOR DORNYEI (Thunder Duo – Stanley Jordan – Worldwide renowned clinician and educator)

“As a DIME Online student, Emanuele consistently delivered high-level work and was awarded a ﬁrst class honors degree. Throughout his studies, he demonstrated excellent technique, musicality, creativity, versatility and a big, solid groove. These qualities, along with his professional work ethic and endless enthusiasm, will no doubt make him an asset to any musical situation he is involved in” MIKE STURGIS (DIME Online Head of Education, Redtenbacher’s Funkestra, Wishbone Ash, Asia, Bob James, Bob Mintzer, Randy Brecker, Courtney Pine, Elton John, David Bowie)

“Emanuele is an affable musician with a big heart! I thoroughly enjoyed the time that I spent working with him and always found him to be inquisitive, diligent and hard-working. I am conﬁdent that he would be an invaluable addition to any musical outﬁt!” FERGUS GERRAND (Sting, Madonna, Spice Girls, Take That, Duran Duran)

“Emanuele stepped into the Wily Bo Walker Acoustic Band and immediately brought a cool and musical aspect to the percussion side of the band. Working either with a full kit or a stripped back percussion setup, depending on the venue, Emanuele brings a keen perspective on what is required for each song. Energetic, enthusiastic and open to ideas, it is always a pleasure to work with a drummer of his talent!” WILY BO WALKER (Scottish solo artist, songwriter, composer, and performer inducted in the U.S. Blues Hall Of Fame as a “Master Blues Artist”)

“As Emanuele’s tutor at DIME Online Degree program, I can testify to his good nature and professionalism. If you’re looking for a versatile, reliable and conscientious drummer, give him a call!” PAUL ELLIOTT (DIME Online Head of Drums, Shaun Baxter, Frank Gambale, Guthrie Govan)

column38:
Hey Rhona, 

As requested see my bio below 😀

My name is Dom Ladd and I am a professional bassist with over 25 years of playing experience from Cuckfield in West Sussex ! 

I studied at ACM Guildford back in 1998 and have also studied with some of the UKs top bassists including Stefan Redtenbacher , Dave Swift and Franc O Shea ! 

I have played all over the U.K. and also Europe from small bars to O2 academy’s (even played the legendary Cavern Club in Liverpool - famous due to some boy band from the 60s 🤣…. 

One of my favourite shows was at the Brighton Centre where I got to play in front of Jimmy Page and Roger Daltrey! I also played in band that toured supporting the Average White Band which was so cool! Gotta love that funk right? 😉😜

I have extensive studio session recording experience and have recorded bass in top studios including Matrix in London (Prince was there the week before we were!!) and Rockfield in Wales (the same room they’ve recorded so many amazing artists and albums …. The most notable being Bohemian Rhapsody!) 

I have performed live on TV and radio as well as playing festivals around the U.K…..

I am very fortunate to also perform alongside some of the UKs top players who regularly play with the UKs top acts like Craig David , Take That , James Bay, Sugarbabes, ELO, Supergrass, Mel C and so many others….. 

My bass playing has been heard on radio 2, 6 music, Kerrang and Planet Rock to name a few and releases by acts on labels such as Skint Records, Tru Thoughts , Earache , Food Records, and Freestyle Records….

I’ve worked with top producers who have released top 10 U.K. singles and remixed tracks for Britney Spears , Usher and Beyoncé. 

I also have played alongside many blues artists including Will Johns (Eric Claptons nephew) , Miller Anderson , Papa George , Ken Emerson, Mark Butcher, and Pete Orr. 

During lockdown I was asked to play bass for the international Alter Bridge Collaboration Project (on YouTube) which was great fun !! 

I use a Fender 64 Jazz and a Fender 50s P bass as well as a Spector Coda 5 deluxe. 

I use DR lo rider strings and a Mark Bass Marcus Miller amp (that has been played by the man himself at Ronnie Scott’s 😎)  along with TC Electronic RS115, RS210 and RS212 cabs. 

I also occasionally use an EBS HD350 with a classic 410 cab and have a pedal board (which is purple and way cool!!) and  use various pedals by TC Electronic, MXR and Boss. 

I currently play in a wedding band called The Mayhems and The Wildchilds. 

I also regularly dep in many wedding and corporate function bands around Brighton and the South East. 

I do BVs ! Which I love!!

Depping all the time has given me the opportunity to build up a vast song list from 50s up to present day in various genres !! I love soul , blues, funk, rock , indie and Motown the best ! 

I’d love to join the Supreme collective and it would be awesome if you’d have me on board! 

It would be great to play some lovely weddings plus more, along with meeting  talented new players and most importantly, lovely people! 

If you need any other info please get in touch. 

All the best 

Dom

Ambitious, diverse and heavily swayed by extraordinary sound, Fred Hills creates captivating compositions by fusing influences from around the musical world. Inspired to play by the unique and revolutionary musicians that preceded him, Fred Hills incorporates his love of jazz, prog, electronica, world and folk music into his colourful rhythms, melodic ‘open-handed’ beats and delicate guitar-based compositions.

Fred has written, recorded & toured with a number of esteemed UK artists including Ellie Ford, Michael Baker, Time for T & Mara Simpson. In 2021, Fred was hired as musical director for multiple award-winning theatre show, 'YOU', which he composed for and performed several sold-out events at Brighton Fringe Festival. Fred regularly performs live with his trio, which consists of producer & multi-instrumentalist Andrew Stuart-Buttle and double bassist Alfie Weedon. 

Fred's debut EP 'Incerto', released via Ninja Tune Production Music, draws influences from The Cinematic Orchestra, White Denim, Jose Gonzales and others. This has been brought to life through collaborative efforts with producers and sound designers; Alex Barron and Aneek Thapar, who share Fred’s vision to create music and sounds that defy genres and cross traditional boundaries. 

Aside from his dedication to original projects, Fred has completed several cruise contracts for P&O, Disney, Tallink & Silja, DFDS &  with various function bands over the years. Fred has also enjoyed jazz band residencies at several high-profile restaurants including Quaglino's, Andaz London & The Wellesley London.

Connor McCorkindale is a freelance drummer based in Brighton UK, with a passion for pop, electronic and rock music. His passion for pop music has allowed him to develop a vast understanding of electronics and hybrid set ups, including Roland sample pads & triggers, playing to backing tracks & click tracks, as well as Logic Pro X & Ableton Live. 

His desire for the instrument has led him to delve deep into different styles & genres, techniques, and advanced concepts to further develop his proficiency as a drummer. Combining his ability as a drummer and his knowledge of electronics and Ableton he has been able to provide musical direction and live playback for many artists.

For over a decade Connor has played for numerous bands and artists (Coco & The Lost, Hourglvss, Latenight Honeymoon) giving him the chance to play, and tour all over the UK including major festivals such as Y NOT and the Isle of Wight, venues such as Camden Roundhouse, Concorde 2, and Liverpool Arts Club, alongside multiple performances on BBC Introducing and the musical “9 to 5”.

Connor has also studied with renowned UK drummer & educator Pat Garvey, who has also taught drummers such as Ben Thompson (Two Door Cinema Club), Deborah Knox-Hewson (Charlie XCX), Ben Thomas (Rag ‘n’ Bone Man), Rufus Taylor (The Darkness) amongst many others.

Gareth 'Gaz Drums' Davies
Gaz began his musical journey at 13 and by the age of 14, he was playing drums in local band. He quickly made a name for himself in the local scene as one of Llanelli's most accomplished players, gaining him the nickname 'Gaz Drums'.
​
Gaz went on to study Popular Music before studying music at degree level at Bournemouth University. After his return to Llanelli, Gaz found his love for teaching the drums. He taught in primary schools in the Cardiff area as well as teaching privately, a practice that has continued to this day. Gaz has taught hundreds of students over the years, some going on to study at heavyweight music universities such as BIMM and ACM. His trademark hat and smile are known well in the music scene and he continues to record and perform with function bands all over the UK.

Born in Huddersfield, a small yet progressive town in the heart of Yorkshire I had a variety of musical influences. Huddersfield carnival was a big part of my childhood and being strongly tied to my Carriacou roots 'big drum' and freestyling rhythm with words and african dance was something I learnt to explore from a very young age with the encouragement of my grandad and great Aunt whom were first generation Windrush and strong leaders of the Carribean community in Huddersfield .

Moving to London just before my teens I discovered the Brit School where I studied music for 3 years and alongside my studies I grew up around alot of grime artists and would record in Tottenham developing my craft , writing choruses for grime artists such as Subten, Potter Payper and Luke Reid (crazy cousins).

Currently I'm writing on upcoming projects for upcoming artists and this year will see many of those releases coming into the light

I have an extensive professional career that spans more than two decades. I play both the drums and percussion at an equal standard and have had the opportunity to tour the world several times and perform with many great world-class artists such as Ricky Martin, Imagination, Jocelyn Brown, Sophie Ellis-Bextor, Leon Ware and many more.

column38:
San Francisco-Native and Southampton-Based David Baron Stevens is a performer that transcends genre and borders, and always leaves his audience feeling something.

He has performed in all sorts of ensembles, from a Brazilian Duo to an 88-piece orchestra and everything in between. DBS has decades of experience in the Broadway world, Jazz Orchestras and Big Bands, Soul and Blues bands, Symphony Orchestras, small Jazz ensembles, Flamenco Duos, and Chamber Orchestras. 

His studies have led him to some of the most prolific performers who are one degree away from the greatest of all time on their instrument, if not a great master themselves, such as Phil Woods (saxophone legend), Kenny Garrett (of the Miles Davis band), Jeff Clayton (of the Clayton-Hamilton Jazz Orchestra), and Tracy Harris (Yamaha Flute Artist and student of Julius Baker of Julliard and James Galway). 

DBS has been a performer and musical director on cruise ships for nearly 20 years and performed all types of popular and classic music. His repertoire contains around 1000 pieces and songs.

3 years of working with B.B. King Blues Club in Memphis, Tennessee had a tremendous impact on his style and professionalism. 

Everywhere DBS goes in the world, he is quickly invited on stage and is among the best musicians in the city. 

When you hear a performance by David Baron Stevens, you will realise that you've heard some of the best saxophone playing you've heard in a long time, and when you perform with him, you'll feel like you've made a new friend for life. 

Today, DBS performs often in London in the jazz clubs of Soho, and in Palermo, Sicily in their jazz clubs and major concert theaters as a featured artist.

JACK MCGAUGHEY IS A PROFESSIONAL MUSICIAN BASED IN CAMBRIDGE, UK.
Born in Eugene, Oregon, USA, Jack traveled across the pond to study at Paul McCartney’s internationally renowned Liverpool Institute for Performing Arts, where he received a Bachelor of Arts in Music with First Class Honours. Jack has honed his craft through studies with leading industry professionals, including Mo Pleasure and Gary Baldwin.

Primarily a keyboardist specializing in Hammond organ, Jack is a versatile musician with a great deal of professional experience. He has recorded in studios like Abbey Road, gigged in iconic venues such as Ronnie Scott’s and The Cavern Club, and played festivals like The Moulin Blues Festival and Liverpool Sound City. 

Jack performs regularly with function bands all over the UK and at sea with Carnival Cruises and Royal Caribbean. He has backed songwriters for sessions hosted by SoFar Sounds and The Blues Kitchen, and his recorded work has featured on a variety of albums, most notably The Craig Charles Trunk of Funk Vol. 2 with PM Warson. Jack continues to tour regularly with several artists all over the world.

I am a bass player based in the West Midlands, and music has been my passion since I was young. I started playing the bass when I was just 14 years old, and since then, it has been a central part of my life. 

One of my biggest strengths as a bass player is my versatility. I am comfortable playing a wide range of styles. I can also read music, which has helped me to work with other musicians.

column38:
Vinicius Colla (Vinnie Colla) is a Brazilian bass player. He was born in Sao Paulo, Brazil.

Colla started learning bass guitar when he was 11 years old. Since then he has focused on studying music and developing his talent and experience as a musician. 
For a long time, he was the pupil of the renowned Brazilian bass player Itamar Collaço, from Zimbo Trio.

As a professional musician, he founded Insônica, one of the most active functions band on the circuit of pubs, bars and clubs in São Paulo. Over 15 years, Colla played, produced and managed the band, they were frequently hired for weddings and corporative events, with about 220 shows per year.

As part of Insônica, Colla has always explored his creativity, using 6-string basses and several bass pedals, and has focused on arrangements of music. In this way, he has created a unique style of playing and covering songs.

He Also played for 4 years in a Musical called "Um Certo Faroeste Caboclo"  at the most important Theaters in Brazil.

Colla has also participated in other projects, in particular focusing on original music and worked in a freelance capacity for music studios, both recording and producing. Among his work are recordings for a Japanese music label, ranging from albums to a live DVD filmed in Japan.

With this vast experience both on stage and in the studio, Colla has mastered playing fretless and 4, 5, 6 or 7-string basses. Living in the UK since 2014, where he continues to develop his musical career. Colla has been playing and recording with different artists in London, and in 2018 he has joined Inglorious, a renowned British rock band.

With Inglorious, Colla recorded three albums, "We Will Ride" (Original), "Heroine" (Cover Album) and a Live DVD Album (XXXXI Live at Phoenix) and has been playing as headliners in the biggest venues in the UK and in Europe (i.e. Shepherds Bush Empire, Islington Assembly Hall, O2 Academy, O2 Bristol, Rock City,  etc.) and in the biggest summer rock festivals in UK and Europe. 

As a session musician, Colla has been playing with some of the best function bands and tribute bands around UK, always delivering the best sound quality, commitment and profissionalism with his diverse set list.

column38:
Born and raised in Moscow, Russia, Vlada Brofman has spent the second part of her life in the US, mostly on the East coast. Fully fluent in English, Russian, and French, she appears equally at home in different musical traditions: world music, jazz, French chanson, and the lyric-centered vision of singer-songwriters… Her primary training in acting (with a Moscow theatre director Semen Rivkin) has been complemented by jazz voice lessons with Berklee-educated voice teachers.

A versatile vocalist and guitarist, Vlada has a “voice of verve and sass” (Shauna Erlbaum, thenoise-boston.com) and on stage projects an utmost attention to the delivery of lyrics and a magnetic charisma. Her interpretations of songs are always sensitive and filled with drama. Each song comes alive with a new persona, giving the listener “a sensation of being at a feast, or witnessing a minor wonder, of which you should remember every second” (Aleksey Kartashov).  

A protean performer, Vlada "transforms before our very eyes, into sultry and cunning Orlandina; then, a fickle Frenchwoman, whose love lasts for only one dance; then, ethereal Amalia before whom a young soldier is kneeling… Vlada’s voice turns your head and entices, tempts and teases, grieves and confides in you” (Slava Gerovich). Turning every song into a mini-play, she also shows great tastefulness in repertoire choices, reimagining well-known songs and, more recently, performing her originals in Russian and English.

column38:
Scottish producer and multi-instrumentalist Ali McLeod is an active performer and educator.

“Music can have a great emotional impact on people,” he says. “Making music is not just about writing songs, playing instruments and performing, it’s about the desire to make a difference in people’s lives.”

Growing up in a household where music was always booming, McLeod started out on guitar and piano at age eight, before becoming fascinated with rhythm and turning his focus to drumming at eleven.

He started formal studies at Edinburgh College, graduating “with Distinction” in Popular Music Performance, and completed his degree at the Institute of Contemporary Music Performance in London, where he was awarded a BMus (Hons) with First Class Honours. In the process, his taste expanded from an initial passion for classic rock and blues to include other genres such as jazz, funk and fusion. He has been particularly inspired by the bands Snarky Puppy and Lettuce.

He studied under multiple Grammy-winning engineer Steve Genewick and Recording Academy 2010 “Producer of the Year” Gregg Field and has produced several artists at Berklee College of Music, Valencia – including Sarah Martinson, Nan MacMillan and Naomi Westwater.  McLeod has also recorded with artists such as Marcelo Woloski and Nona Hendryx. 

“Ali is a great musician and a very tenacious, hard-working person,” said pianist and composer Polo Orti.

McLeod strives to perfect each artist’s vision in both his productions and performances, and enjoys working with creative artists in a wide array of styles.

As a professional drummer, I have played with countless Rock, Pop and R&B bands, orchestras, theatre productions and ensembles all over the UK whilst also currently teaching drums at ‘Rock Out!’ in Chorlton, Manchester.

After winning the Greene King Battle of the Bands, my old band Skies Turn Black would regularly perform Pop and Classic Rock covers at pubs, bars and birthday events all over the UK, everywhere from Milton Keyes to Wakefield to Manchester. We were paid £250 per gig with regular songs such as ‘Mr Brightside’ by The Killers, ‘I Believe in a Thing Called Love’ by The Darkness, ‘Shape of You’ by Ed Sheeran, ‘Girls Just Want To Have Fun’ by Cyndi Lauper, ‘Take On Me’ by a-ha and ‘Don’t Stop Me Now’ by Queen being particular popular tracks that went down well with audiences dancing, singing along and sometimes participating in songs on stage.

I have a wide range of performance experience, can play to click/backing track live, have full gear and my own transport and can be free for all gigs. I am confident, hard-working, punctual, sociable and dedicated to providing an enriching experience for the audience and having fun!

I am a 20 year old bass guitarist and cellist (grade 8 distinction), and have grown up in a family of musicians. My dad is a pianist, among other things, and studied at the Royal Academy of Music in London. He taught me music from a young age; I have great music theory and can read sheet music proficiently.

I taught myself bass and soon joined my secondary school's senior jazz band; the band had a large repertoire with over 50 pieces. We would play around 20+ gigs throughout the school year, some to schools and some to large audiences. We also would do an annual tour of Derbyshire, playing at several large venues such as Chatsworth House. 

Since leaving school, I have played several gigs for a friend of mine who is a songwriter. I also recently stepped in for an ill bassist on very short notice to play a gig at the Lansdowne members club in Mayfair which was a great experience that I throughly enjoyed.

I am very committed and believe I have skills to offer. 

Thanks,
Ben Wood

column38:
Originally from Cape Town, South Africa, now based in London UK, Mike Horne was already well established as one of South Africa's most In-Demand, and Respected session drummers, and was often referred to as one of the countries "Most Recorded Drummers", before his talents took him abroad. 

- Mike studied a BMus Degree, in Jazz Performance, at the University Of Cape Town. He was soon handpicked for the Universities top student ensembles, The University Jazz Big Band, and The Latin Tentet. He also toured nationally with The South African National Youth Big Band, and was one of the founding members of the elite South African National Youth Jazz Band, in 2001 & again in 2002.

- Mike has recorded and performed with most of South Africas most respected Artists, including LOYISO, Freshlyground, Danny K, Winston Mankunku Ngozi, Judith Sephuma, Vicky Sampson, The Parlotones, Elvis Blue, Fokofpolisiekar, Van Coke Kartel, aKing, Arno Carstens, Lloyd Cele, Ard Matthews, Toya Delazy, Neville D, Watershed, Tumi And The Volume, Locnville, Zahara, Micasa, Macstanley, Hog Hoggidy Hog, 7thSon, Chris Chameleon, Louise Carver, Koos Kombuis, Dozi, Kurt Darren, Mike Rossi, Carlo Mombelli, Marcus Wyatt.

- Mike has worked with international artists including, Harold Japhta (SA/SWDN), Ron McCurdy (USA), Bill Prince (USA), Bruce Cassidy (Canada), Jack Van Poll (NDL) Hein Van De Guyn (NDL), Koos Mark (NDL), Lee Gold (UK) Earl Okin (UK), Steve Waterman (UK), Tristan Keyte (UK), eVoid (UK), and Massimo Morganti (Italy, Horacio Hernandez, Bob Mintzer).

- Mike has  shared stages with the likes of Dianne Schuur, Gerald Albright, Courtney Pine, Lagwagon, The Mad Caddies, NoFx, Foreigner, Counting Crows, Collective Soul, Seether and Metallica.

- Mike has also toured SA nationally, as the drummer for Grammy Award winning, USA Rock ‘n Roll legend, ED ROLAND of COLLECTIVE SOUL.

- Since relocating to London UK, Mike's career has gone from strength to strength, and so far has worked with the likes of American Guitar Legend JOSH SMITH (Joe Bonamassa, Mick Jagger) the Godfather of British Neo-Soul OMAR Lyefook MBE. 
Disco Soul legends ODYSSEY,  Robin Banerjee (Amy Winehouse), Acantha Lang, Chris Jerome (Mica Paris), Kevin Glasgow (Tim Garland, Jason Robello), Jay Phelps, eVOID, Brandon Allen, Vasillis Xenopoulous, Quentin Collins, Steve Waterman, Fulvio Sigurta, Steve Fishwick, british jazz legend Dave O'Higgins, Ferris & Sylvester, Francis Hylton (incognito), Derek Nash (Jools Holland Orchestra), Winston Blisset (Massive Attack), Michael Coates, Lisa Mann, Noel Mcalla (Mezzoforte), Rob Hughes and many more...

column38:
Chris Cox is a freelance drummer currently based in Southampton, UK. From 2014-2018, he studied at the University of Leeds where he completed a BA in Music with Enterprise and an MA in Music and Management (First-Class Honours). During his time at Leeds, he performed drums in an array of musical ensembles such as the LUU Big Band and Musical Theatre societies. During his studies, he built a wide repertoire of musical genres making him a versatile player for big bands, small band Jazz ensembles, theatre as well as rock/pop party bands. 

Initially based in Yorkshire, Chris went on to become a well established freelance player where he has focused on high end function work and party band ensembles. He has regularly performed at corporate company events for the likes of the BBC, ITV, Soho House, AO UK and Manchester United FC. 

Chris has performed across the UK and Europe on tours, festivals, radio and television. His diverse performance resume includes the B-52s 40th Anniversary tour (main support), BBC Sports Personality of the Year, (Utility Arena), BBC Breakfast, ITV National, Capital Radio (London) Cork Jazz Festival (Ireland) and Lost Village Festival UK. 

Chris has regularly performed in theatre ensembles for various concerts and productions. This includes Camp Bestival (Isle of Wight) The Fringe Festival (Edinburgh) The Howard Goodall Showcase (Westminster Abbey) and the West End production of Mamma Mia with Royal Caribbean. 

Now based in the south of England, he is currently focusing on music tutoring and freelance work.

Hi! My name is Ciro, and I am a London-based singer and performer with more than ten years of experience in national and international tours, festivals, weddings, and function gigs. During these years, I’ve had the opportunity to grow professionally and travel the world doing what I love most, singing and entertaining people. I have performed in front of small and large audiences and collaborated in large productions as part of a five ensemble and as a sonata by learning the various layers involved in organising a show. One of my most outstanding achievements is performing in front of the Princess of Monaco.

I will use my previous experience, exciting energy, and contagious irony to create a show that will remain on everyone’s lips for years to come!

Another thing I’m sure of is my repertoire which should please many generations of music lovers, from the greats of the 60s, 70s, 80s, and 90s, Motown, and dance floor fillers to pop and rock at the top of the modern-day charts. A few names to mention such as Stevie Wonder, Kings of Leon, Bruno Mars, the Beatles, Michael Jackson, George Michael, the Temptations, and more!

My extensive repertoire is from Soul, Funk, Motown, Pop, and Rock to Disco and Dance in three different languages (English, Italian and Spanish).

Look at my song list, and don’t worry if you don’t find a particular song. I may have already heard it; otherwise, I could prepare it specifically for your event.

 

Don't hesitate to ask for a free consultation, your dream entertainment is only one step away from coming true!

Having studied on the prestigious Joint Course between the Royal Northern College of Music and The University of Manchester, Dan graduated with a first-class honours degree in music. Maintaining a keen interest in jazz, musical theatre and classical music genres enables Dan to regularly perform at functions, weddings, shows and conferences around the UK both as a soloist and alongside various ensembles.

Dan is a founding member of the Spark Duo, Arcus Saxophone Quartet and Spectrum Saxes. He relishes the opportunity these groups provide to nurture a diverse musical palate which bridges the gap between the classical and jazz worlds. Alongside recording sessions across the North West, Dan’s career has led him to performances at London’s Wigmore Hall, Nottingham’s Royal Concert Hall, Manchester’s Band on the Wall and multiple appearances live on BBC Radio.

Maria Hernandez is a session singer, a recording artist and a songwriter based in London. She has been singing in function bands since she was 16 years old.  Maria has performed on TV, and radio, at private events, weddings, and festivals, on big stages as well as in intimate venues. She is comfortable singing in full bands as well as acoustic bands, trios, and duos. Maria can sing in English and Spanish. As she has worked in function bands that played different music genres, she is confident in engaging the audience and singing many genres like pop, R&B, ballads, Soul, Dance, Latin, etc. 

As a recording artist, she combines Commercial Pop with Latin, Spanish and Electronic Music influences. Through her songs, she sends a clear message of empowerment, strength, and positivity combining English with touches of Spanish. In recent years, she has worked with worldwide-known artists and producers like Cher, Mark Taylor (Cher, Enrique Iglesias), Chris Zadley (J Balvin),
Siderland, Jeff Retro, Pink Panda and Marco Dalla Villa (La Francesita). In 2022, She was nominated to the Hollywood Music in Media Awards.

Magazines like Mente Lider (Mexico), Music News, Lock Magazine and LionHeart TV (USA/UK) and LH Mag (Spain), Radio stations like The BBC, BBC Introducing, La Cadena SER, Onda Cero and los40; newspapers like El País, and El diari de Tarragona, and TV programmes like ‘Tot es mou’(TV3, Spain) and Aquí entre Nos (Azteca 1, Mexico) , are a few examples of media outlets that have supported her. She has played a number of international performances in Spain, the UK, and the USA (LA, Chicago, and New York) and has played in legendary venues such as O2 Shepherds Bush Empire, Proud Camden and Cargo to name a few.

She has performed at venues throughout the UK and further afield, including The Beacon Theater (Broadway, NYC), The Other Palace (West End, London), Matt & Phred’s (Manchester), Albert Schloss (Liverpool), and Crazy Coqs (London). In 2022, Sara launched her own jazz and blues ensemble, Smoke & Honey, performing music from Ella Fitzgerald to Norah Jones to Bonnie Raitt at jazz bars, weddings and private functions across the UK.

Having spent most of her early adulthood immersed in contemporary vocal music, Sara has worked with companies and ensembles such as the BBC, Universal Music Group, VOCES8, Band on the Wall, and Varsity Vocals USA as arranger, performer, producer, and director. A rising star in the Afrobeats scene, Josh has worked with some of the biggest names in the industry at only 25 years old. Originally hailing from South Wales, he has established himself as a go-to session player in the London music scene.

Josh's multi-genre sound combines Hip-Hop, Afrobeats, Jazz, Soul, R&B, Blues, Pop, and other styles. His wide range of influences are expressed through his musicianship, no sound is off limits.
