Chris Cox is an English freelance drummer based in both the UK and Europe. From 2014-2018, he studied at the University of Leeds where he completed a BA in Music with Enterprise (Upper Second-Class Honours) and an MA in Music and Management (Distinction). During his time at Leeds, he performed drums in an array of musical ensembles such as the LUU Big Band and Musical Theatre societies. During his studies, he built a wide repertoire of musical genres making him a versatile player for big bands, small band Jazz ensembles, theatre as well as rock/pop party bands. 

Initially based in Yorkshire, Chris went on to become a well established freelance player where he has focused on high end function work and party band ensembles. He has performed at corporate company events for the likes of the BBC, ITV, Soho House, AO UK and Manchester United FC. 

Chris has performed across the UK and Europe on tours, festivals, radio and television. His diverse performance resume includes the B-52s 40th Anniversary tour (main support), BBC Sports Personality of the Year, (Utility Arena), BBC Breakfast, ITV National, Capital Radio (London) Cork Jazz Festival (Ireland) and Lost Village Festival UK. 

Chris has regularly performed in theatre ensembles for various concerts and productions. This includes Camp Bestival (Isle of Wight) The Fringe Festival (Edinburgh) The Howard Goodall Showcase (Westminster Abbey) and the West End production of Mamma Mia with Royal Caribbean. 

He is currently focusing on freelance work and is available for both UK and international dep gigs.

I signed a publishing deal with Sony music, and worked with many Pop, Garage, RnB and Soul artists, producing and performing at festivals all over the UK, and at all the prestigious music venues in London and the UK. I also wrote and produced music for BBC and Channel 4 TV programmes, as well as Managing up and coming artists.

Qualified as a teacher in 2005, I have taught music performance and technology at various colleges in London, whilst still continuing to work as a session musician, performing with many up coming as well as top UK and US artists including Bobby V, The Drifters, David Joseph, Omar, Shola Ama, Big Bruuvaz, Kofi among many others, and working alongside some the countries top musicians.

I have produced many successful albums and singles over the years in various genres, and relishes working with new and up and coming artists.

Sophie is a freelance saxophonist based in London. She graduated from the Guildhall School of Music and Drama with a BMus (Hons) in Saxophone Performance in 2019. 

She takes on regular performances with award-winning function bands, such as 29 Fingers, She's Got Brass, Das Brass, Blue Lion Band, She Music Global, alongside many solo functions too. Sophie also has an accomplished sax and percussion duo, as well as a sax, percussion and vocals trio in addition to her solo work through Encore. If interested in her duo or trio, don't hesitate to enquire now!

In addition to her function work, she also takes on regular jazz, afrobeat, Berber and hip-hop gigs, touring with MC Solomon's band, performing at festivals with Arfoud Brothers and Sisters and Berber Diffusion, hosting jam nights with Inverted Worlds, and playing more traditional jazz with the London Jazz Orchestra.

She has over a decade of professional performing and recording experience, in every genre imaginable - from classical, jungle, afrobeat and pop!

Sophie can provide a full audio setup for smaller performances with her own microphones, laptop and PA system, as well as being a wireless roaming saxophonist for performances if required.

Emma Edel is an emerging independent Pop artist hailing from Kerry, currently based in London. With her original music, she has supported Caoi De Barra in St Michael’s Church, performed alongside Robert Grace for iRadio’s iLoveIrish Live, supported Lyra in the INEC Club, opened for True Tides in Mike The Pies & supported Skinny Living at The Soundhouse. She also took to the stage for a sell-out performance of her first hometown headline show at St Michael's Church in March with a full band performance, as well as doing showcases and open mics all around London. Her most recent performance include The Forge, at The Lower Third and the main stage at the Cahersiveen Festival of Music. 

She is the lead singer with The Funky Waves, a 4 piece party band performing a mix of soul, pop, funk, r&b and rock and also performs acoustic sets throughout London. She previously played keys and backing vocals for a 7 piece event band and has sang and played acoustic guitar/piano in smaller acoustic bands. She has her own in ear monitoring system & a wireless mic and extensive experience singing for weddings, parties, events, bars & pubs. 


Emma Edel’s debut single ‘If I Can’t Have You’ was released at the end of 2020 and it peaked at No.4 on the Irish Radio Breakers chart, hit No.40 on the Irish Shazam charts and surpassed 4 million impacts to date on Irish radio. Throughout 2021, she released three singles, ‘Meant To Be’, ‘Good Time’ & ‘Why We Try’, gaining the singer traction from publications such as Hot Press, The Last Mixed Tape & Golden Plec and gaining significant radio play and track of the week slots from the likes of iRadio & Spin SW. She released ‘I Reckon Something’ and ‘On My Mind’ last year and both landed on the Irish Radio Breakers Chart. Her music has been played on radio stations across the country such as Today FM, RTÉ 2FM, Spin 1038, Spin SW, iRadio, FM104, Beat 102 103, Cool FM, Radio Kerry, KCLR & Red FM to name a few and has featured in The Irish Independent, Star Chic Magazine & Le Future Wave.

Tom has worked as a professional drummer since 2011, during which time he has worked in multiple areas of the music industry. Performance experience includes hundreds (if not thousands) of shows ranging from function work to theatre shows, festivals and pubs/clubs. He is also a member of the Vic Firth Education Team and a Roland Tutor Ambassador and an endorsee of Evans drumheads, Protection Racket cases and Porter and Davies monitoring systems.

column38:
Hey Rhona,

Hope you are well? 

Please see my bio below as requested…..

My name is Dom Ladd, and I am a professional bassist with over 25 years of playing experience from Cuckfield in West Sussex. 

I studied at ACM Guildford back in 1998 and have also studied with some of the UKs top bassists including Stefan Redtenbacher, Dave Swift, and Franc O Shea. 

I have played all over the U.K. and Europe from small bars to O2 academy’s (even played the legendary Cavern Club in Liverpool - famous due to some boy band from the 60s…..I was in New York at the end of 2023 for 8 weeks and played some shows around bars which was amazing! 

One of my favourite shows was at the Brighton Centre where I got to play in front of Jimmy Page and Roger Daltrey! I also played in band that toured supporting the Average White Band which was so cool! Gotta love that funk right? 😉😜

I have extensive studio session recording experience and have recorded bass in top studios including Matrix in London (Prince was there the week before we were!!) and Rockfield in Wales (the same room they’ve recorded so many amazing artists and albums …. The most notable being Bohemian Rhapsody!) 

I have performed live on TV and radio as well as playing festivals around the U.K…..

I am very fortunate to also perform alongside some of the UKs top players who regularly play with the UKs top acts like Craig David, Take That, James Bay, Sugarbabes, ELO, Supergrass, Mel C and so many others….. 

My bass playing has been heard on radio 2, 6 music, Kerrang and Planet Rock to name a few and releases by acts on labels such as Skint Records, Tru Thoughts, Earache, Food Records, and Freestyle Records….

I’ve worked with top producers who have released top 10 U.K. singles and remixed tracks for Britney Spears, Usher, and Beyoncé. 

I also have played alongside many blues artists including David Bennet Cohen (Mick Taylor, Jimi Hendrix, Huey Lewis, Janis Joplin), Will Johns (Eric Claptons nephew), Miller Anderson, Papa George, Ken Emerson, Mark Butcher, and Pete Orr. 

During lockdown I was asked to play bass for the international Alter Bridge Collaboration Project (on YouTube) which was great fun!! 

I use a Fender 64 Jazz and a Fender 50s P bass as well as a Spector Coda 5 deluxe. 

I use DR strings and a Mark Bass Little Marcus 800 (which was used at the Jazz Café by Marcus Miller himself!) with TC Electronic RS210 and RS212 cabs. 

I also occasionally use an EBS HD350 with a classic 410 cab and use various pedals by TC Electronic, MXR and Boss. 

I dep in many wedding and corporate function bands around London, Brighton and the South East. 

I also do BVs! Which I love!!

Depping all the time has given me the opportunity to build up a vast song list from 50s up to present day in various genres…. I love soul, blues, funk, rock, indie and Motown. 

I’d love to join the Supreme Collective and feel I would be an asset to your already strong team! 

If you need any other info please get in touch. 

All the best 

Dom 

I’d also love to play more weddings!! I freaking love playing at weddings!!!

column38:
Gonçalo's first musical steps were taken quite early. While still in primary school he had piano and theory initiation for two years.
Later, he studied guitar intensely to develop a solid knowledge of the basics and beyond. By the time he was 17, he was already gigging regularly in small groups. For the next few years, his dedication to the instrument kept growing and he feverishly kept learning different genres while developing his own style.
By 20 years old he focused on studying and playing Jazz and started a Jazz Performance Degree at JBJazz, a private jazz school in Lisbon, where he studied with some of the best musicians in Portugal. A highlight of this time was performing at the Hot Club of Lisbon, performing the music of Bernardo Moreira, one of Portugal’s great Jazz masters.
At 24 he moved to London to attend an undergraduate degree in Popular Music Performance at the University of East London. During this time, he was introduced to a whole new level of musicianship through his teachers, his colleagues, and the people he worked with. Playing with different artists, embracing new instruments and skills, such as piano, and percussion, arranging skills, recording, and releasing 2 full-length albums, working with the label Dreamscope Records, and gigging regularly, his appreciation and love for music and guitar was more focused than ever. 
At 30, Gonçalo decided to embark on his biggest challenge yet, completing a master’s degree at the prestigious Guildhall, where a very high level of musicianship was demanded of him.
At present, Gonçalo is as dedicated as ever, taking on every challenge that the future brings with love and dedication.

column38:
Born in the vibrant town of Brighton, Raena trained in musical theater from a young age. Music has always been an essential part of her life, but her journey into music has not been conventional. Growing up around the free festival scene, Raena was freestyling over DNB and Jungle from a young age and started out making liquid DNB records. However, Raena’s true musical passion is soul, jazz and hip-hop with influences such as Erykah Badu, Amy Winehouse, Souls of Mischief, Hiatus Kaiyote and Nina Simone.

Raena has released two singles ‘See The Light’ and ‘Weighs Down’ both well received by supporters and local radio stations. These songs are from a previous collab with producer Os Price and have a sultry hip-hop feel with neo-soul melodic vocals. Since the release of these singles, Raena has spent a lot of time crafting and producing her sound with more control over the entire creative process, leading to her latest collection of demos. After spending time in the vibrant up and coming jazz scene in Brighton, Reana was able to share her work with a band of jazz musicians and together they are recording this exciting new project entitled ‘Higher Planes’. This project is the introduction of her sound to the world and the industry. With infectious vocal hooks, live drums and jazz influenced instrumentals, ‘Higher Planes’ is the revisiting of memories & detangling trauma, questioning current issues and finding solace in melodic flows. Recording with a full live band including improvisational live takes as well as fresh new songs this EP is one to look for in 2024. Think Voodoo, Mummas Gun, Frank.

As well as putting on her own spoken word and female led music events, Raena has performed in many venues, supporting local artists, at open mics, as well as hip-hop cyphers and jazz jams in Brighton and London, also at festivals including Boomtown.

Andy Basiola is guitarist with over 20 years of experience, based in London, UK. Andy started playing guitar at the age of 12 growing up listening to artists such as Ozzy Osbourne, Led Zeppelin, Deep Purple, Whitesnake, Mr.Big, Joe Satriani, Eric Johnson, etc...

In 2009 Andy moved to London, after winning a scolarship to study at Bimm Music school, one of Europe’s leading music college, where he graduated in 2010. Later, in 2018, Andy graduated from Falmouth University, with a First Class Honours Bachelor degree in Creative Music performance.

Over the years Andy has played and toured Europe with different bands such as top function band Sound With Us, performing a wide array of styles such as: pop, funk, rock, country, etc.. and playing at events all over the country.

Andy also played some of London’s iconic venues, such as: 02 Shepherds Bush Empire, 02 Islington, 100 Club , Beck Theatre, Barfly, Underworld,  with original bands: Future Shock, Indya, Kill the Kid and acoustic duo Full Blown Acoustic

In the studio, Andy has also worked with producer Phil Kinman (Doogie White, Tank, Driven), and Ade Emsley (Iron Maiden, Steve Harris, Voodoo Six).
In 2022 he had the pleasure of recording at the world famous Abbey Road Studio 2.

In 2012 Andy was voted Best Individual Performer, by Playmusic Magazine, and in the same year he received a guitar endorsement deal with Fret King Guitars, and he is still featured on the company website as an artist.

In 2014 Andy released his first instrumental track called :”Die To Live Again”, and his debut album in 2018
In the same year he became the guitarist for Skid Raw, UK’s top Skid Row tribute band and in 2015 he joined Europe's top Aerosmith Tribute Band "Toxic Twins"

Andy is an experienced guitar teacher and teaches regularly, one to one or group lessons.

A little story about me (I'll try to make it quick :):

I started playing guitar at the age of 12 thanks to my dad, listening to guitarists such as Randy Rhoads, Jimi Hendrix, Gary Moore, Eric Johnson, Joe Satriani, etc… In 2009 I moved to London, after winning a scholarship to study at Bimm Music School, one of Europe’s leading music colleges, where I graduated in 2010.

Over the years I have played and toured Europe with several different bands and played some of London’s iconic venues, such as 02 Shepherds Bush Empire, 02 Islington, 100 Club, Beck Theatre, Barfly, Underworld and many more

In London, I have been writing and recording music with rock bands Kill for Eden and Future Shock, and also worked with producer Phil Kinman (Doogie White, Tank, Driven), and Ade Emsley (Iron Maiden, Steve Harris, Voodoo Six), and TOMMY DE CARLO, lead singer of American rockers BOSTON.

In 2022 I had the pleasure of recording at the the world-famous Abbey Road Studio 2

In 2012 I was voted Best Individual Performer, by Playmusic Magazine, in the same year I received a guitar endorsement deal with Fret-King Guitars.

In 2014 I was selected to take part in the “Vivaldi Metal Project” album, a project involving musicians from all over the world including Rick Wakeman (Yes), Mark Boals, Michael Lepond (Symphony x), Atma Anur and many more.

I have also been the lead guitarist of Aerosmith Rocks UK, one of UK's best Aerosmith tribute bands, since 2017.

I have been performing with function band Sound With Us at events across the UK since 2018

Martin is an experienced and respected drummer and percussionist based in London (UK). He is working alongside some great artists in London and across the UK and has established himself as a reliable, hard working and trustworthy musician. He graduated from The Academy of Contemporary Music (ACM) With a BMus (1st class honours) and was also awarded ‘Outstanding drummer of the year’ out of his graduating year group.
 
Martin has worked, both live and in the studio, with original artists such as Blue Violet, Martha St Arthur, Leddra Chapman, William Poyer, Lauren Ray, Jack Francis, Tessa Violet and Kyiki (Ellie Fletcher) to name a few (see ‘Credits’).

In addition to session work, Martin works within theatre and musical theatre, including The History of Soul Show (Soul Street Productions) the Someone Like You-The Adele Songbook tour (Chameleon Music Marketing Ltd), which has taken him all around the UK and Ireland. He has also  been a member of the house band for the new musical theatre workshop night, Signal as well as part of the band for the workshops of A Small Life (Vin Goodwin) and The Season (Jim Barne and Kit Buchan). 

Finally, he also plays with a number of function/corporate bands. He is a full time member of AKA Party Band, The Beat Freaks, as well as a fill-in drummer for bands such as Coyote, The Cheers and The Pulse amongst many others.

Hey! I'm Alex! 

Academy of Contemporary Music Graduate, multi instrumentalist (Drummer, Guitarist, Percussion, Vocalist + some bass guitar). I have over 20 years experience playing and performing music to people across the U.K.

I've been performing all of my life. I first started playing music at 5 years old. I've been surrounded by music since I was a much smaller human and have performed in bands since I was 12 years old.

I bring an eclectic mix of styles to any occasion, from stripped back acoustic, sing-a-long, Duo and Trio experiences through to high-energy anthems with full bands. I thrive off of audiences and people enjoying themselves, and my goal is always to make sure people are smiling, laughing and having a great time they'll remember!

I know how important memorable performances can be to an occasion. I pride myself on top-tier professionalism, attention to detail and ultimately making sure the clients are entirely satisfied with the event. The job is only ever done when they're happy, not just when the music stops.

column38:
Hi! I’m Joe, and I’m a musician and songwriter who plays at events ranging from weddings to birthdays to corporate parties and everything in between.

I play upbeat, happy covers spanning from 50s to present day. My set consists of classic hit songs with unique verve and energy which get crowds singing and dancing along! Genres include pop/rock/indie/R and B/hip hop/singer/-songwriter and loads more.

In terms of weddings, I’ve got experience playing at every part of the day, from drinks receptions to wedding breakfasts, evening parties and various other configurations of these. I always provide setlists for you in advance, which I build up (pre-booking) based on just a few recommendations from you – this saves you time, and ensures you know that you’re getting what you want from me on the day. I’m very adept at reading crowds too, so on the day I make sure to be flexible and ensure that I’m going with the mood that you and your wedding party are in.

Personalisation is really important to me – I want to ensure that your day is as unique and special as your events and relationships are. I’ve co-ordinated plenty of surprise performances before including engagements (she said yes), so you’re in safe hands with me if this is what you’re planning. I’m also more than happy to learn new songs for you to make sure you hear any particular favourites – I’ve even learned songs in other languages before (so if you want to hear some Turkish music from the 90s, you’re in luck). 

In terms of other events, I’ve played all over the UK and in Europe ranging from huge corporate parties for companies like Morgan Motors, WeWork and HSBC, small and large birthday parties, surprise engagements, pub gigs and pretty much everything in between. In originals gigs, I’ve played at the Adelphi Theatre, Indigo at O2, Wilton’s Music Hall and loads more. I even once played on stage with Maroon 5 as well! Because of all of this experience I’m confident playing in front of any crowd size, so whether there are 10 or 10,000 people attending your event, rest assured that I can handle it!

I’m also a producer and singer-songwriter who’s had original music played on BBC Radio 1 (on Jack Saunders’ Future Artist sShow), BBC Radio 6, Made In Chelsea and more – so if you’re looking for a personalised song, you’re in capable hands (and if you’re looking for a recording, I can produce these to a professional standard for you too).

column38: Emilia Anastazja writes soulful songs that blend R&B with hints of folk, pop, and jazz. Since her debutalbum "Blue," which was released in 2019 and received enormous media coverage, she has established herself in the music industry. Raised in a musical family and trained as a concert pianist from a young age, Emilia's love for music runs deep. Influenced by soul, jazz, and classical music during her formative years, she seamlessly incorporates these elements into her distinctive musical style and songwriting today.In her lyrics she explores themes of love, loss, courage, and self-discovery. With a delicate yet powerful voice, the Polish-Swiss artist captivates her audience effortlessly. As a singer, guitarist, and songwriter, she collaborates with numerous musicians worldwide, extending beyond her solo projects. Emilia has lent her talents to tracks featuring artists such as Lil Durk and contributed to the soundtrack of the recently released film "Creed 3" on J. Cole's label, "Dreamville". Furthermore, her collaborations with Swiss producer Audio Dope have amassed millions of streams on platforms like Spotify. Emilia recently unveiled her new single from her upcoming EP planned for summer 2024. It's a soulful and warm song blending elements of folk and R&B, exploring themes of self-belief and the enduring pursuit of inner tranquility. This release provides a captivating glimpse into an artist honing her identity and realizing her authentic artistic vision.

Hi there!

I'm Alex, 25 years old, from Middlesbrough. I studied in Manchester and split my time between there and London. 

I have been touring Europe the last 4 years with Johnny Cash Roadshow, playing major theatres across the continent, in which I joined whilst completing my studies at the Royal Northern College of Music. I graduated in 2022 with the highest recital grade as a drummer in the history of the university. I won a national drumming competition with hundreds of entrants in 2021, Yamaha Play Along competition, in which I won £1000 and a private masterclass with Steve White (The Who/ Paul Weller).

I have experience in all types of bands, playing to audiences of 2000+, every genre from Motown, Country, Pop, Soul, Metal, Jazz... I have developed a unique play-style which I adapt to be as musically complimentary as possible for all playing situations. I am also a confident backing vocalist, having sung backing vocals for JCR as well as a full lead verse, which is one of the clips in my promo that I linked.

I'm looking to play with new musicians this year, especially in the London area, expand my repertoire and push myself in different directions musically. I see the supreme collective as the ideal band for me to do this, having heard very positive things about the reputation of the band. I am confident in my ability in all live scenarios and would love to show that as part of TSC. I would be happy to audition in person or jump straight into a gig. 

Hope to hear from you, thanks so much!

Find more about Yuri Kufa on YouTube and Instagram.

Coming from a musical family, his father also a drummer, James began learning the drums and piano in 1993, aged 8.
 
Choosing to focus on the drums as a teenager, James became involved with many different groups including Rock, Pop and Jazz bands, and classical music orchestras and ensembles.
 
Discovering his passion lay in music whilst at University, James surrounded himself with as much music as possible, covering many styles: Jazz, Funk, Pop, Rock, Soul/RnB, Latin, Hip-Hop, Drum n Bass and more.
 
In 2008, James began studying with renowned drum teacher Bob Armstrong, who has taught many of the top UK players.  This made a huge impact on James' playing and has been invaluable in all areas of his musicianship.
 
Today, with a vast amount of experience as a professional musician, James finds himself busy with many different projects covering all styles of music, from original groups/artists and function bands, to Big Band and small group jazz projects, military style Drumlines and studio/session work.  Theatre work is a regular feature as well, and James has played on shows including Evita, Jesus Christ Superstar, Avenue Q, The Wedding Singer, Our House, The Wiz and many more.
 
James has travelled all over the UK and Europe, playing venues and events including The Montreux Jazz Festival, Ronnie Scotts Jazz Club, Twickenham Stadium (for the Rugby World Cup 2015), Cadogan Hall, Pizza Express Jazz Club (Soho), Knebworth Park and many more.
James has played live and on recordings for BBC Radio, Jazz FM, Freestyle Records, and Imagem Production Music.

About Me:
Hello! My name is Rocco Roberto Macchia, and I am a dedicated and passionate professional musician specializing in both electric and double bass. With a career spanning over a decade, I bring a wealth of experience, versatility, and enthusiasm to every performance.

Experience and Background:
I began my musical journey in 2007, quickly finding my footing in live performances. By 2010, I had established myself as a professional musician. Since 2020, I have been working full-time in the music industry, which includes working at BIMM, where I also earned my degree in Popular Music Performance. 

Musical Expertise:
My musical repertoire is incredibly diverse, encompassing over 600 songs across genres such as rock, pop, soul, jazz, R&B, Motown, funk, metal, and punk. This versatility allows me to cater to a wide range of audiences and events, ensuring that every performance is tailored to the specific needs and preferences of the clients.

Professional Equipment and Setup:
I come fully prepared with my own transportation and top-notch equipment, including amps and in-ear monitors (IEM). Additionally, I own a high-quality PA system and lighting setup, ensuring that every performance not only sounds great but also looks spectacular.

Performance Highlights:
Throughout my career, I have had the privilege of playing at various functions, corporate events, and weddings. I have also performed with renowned artists, leading to tours and festival appearances across Europe, the UK, and the UAE. These experiences have honed my ability to deliver outstanding performances in diverse settings.


I am not just a musician; I am a team player and a problem solver. With over five years of experience playing in party bands and managing my own function band, I understand the intricacies of sound checks, client expectations, and band dynamics. My proactive approach ensures seamless coordination and top-quality performances. Additionally, I am more than happy to offer transportation for fellow band members.

Hi, my name's Tom. I'm a drummer currently based in South Wales.
I've worked as a pro drummer for the last seven years playing in everything from party bands to theatre pits, as well as backing notable performers such as Gareth Gates, Joe McElderry, Kerry Ellis and James Argent to name a few. I have my own transport and pro grade gear.
I'm happy to provide my CV and references upon request.

I'm DJ Green, and for over a decade now, I've been spinning tracks across every genre you can imagine. I've got this knack for reading crowds and blending different musical styles that keeps the energy flowing and the dance floor packed.
In my 10+ years behind the decks, I've done it all - from organising intimate parties to playing at concerts and everything in between. I love the thrill of putting together an event from scratch and watching it come to life.
I've also had the incredible opportunity to share my love for music over the airwaves as a radio DJ. There's something special about connecting with listeners and curating the perfect playlist that keeps them tuned in.
I'm a full-time software engineer and project manager. It might seem like an odd combo, but I find that the precision and creativity I use in my tech job actually complement my DJ skills perfectly. 
I'm always pushing myself to innovate and excel. 

So that's me - DJ by night, tech whiz by day, and music lover 24/7

Recognized by London Jazz News for his 'vocal quality,
confidence, and ease on stage,' Matt Kent is leaving his mark on
the music industry. His groundbreaking series, "Turning
Classical Music into Pop Songs" has taken TikTok by storm,
amassing an impressive 40 million views and cultivating a
devoted following of over 300,000 across various social media
platforms. In 2019, Kent's talent caught the attention of
Grammy-nominated music producer Dante Lattanzi, leading to a
partnership with Lattanzi's esteemed management company, Caelum
Music Production. The release of his single "Lacrimosa" in 2021
further solidified his rising prominence, achieving an
impressive 12 million streams and countingKent's songwriting and
performance has propelled him to success in numerous
international music competitions, including “The Coffee Music
Project Competition”, “A Singing Heart” hosted by New York
Encounter, and the “Gwangju Busking World Cup”.Matt Kent is in
Collaboration with pianist and songwriter Liv Lester, whom he
met in New York in 2015, Kent and Lester embarked on a
captivating UK tour in 2022 and during this tour caught the
attention of legendary UK radio host Chris Evans, leading to a
momentous opportunity to open for James Bay at CarFest South,
captivating an audience of over 40,000 attendees. Their
partnership continues to flourish, gracing renowned venues in
iconic cities such as London and New York, including esteemed
establishments like Ronnie Scott's, The Bedford, and The Bitter
End.

column38:
The Solomons-Wise Simcha Band provide bespoke music for a large range of Jewish events including weddings and Bar/Bat-Mitzvahs as well as corporate events such as charity fundraisers and conferences.

We specialise in a range of genres from traditional simcha and klezmer music and Israeli dancing to jazz, pop and rock covers. Because of this, the Solomons-Wise Simcha Band can provide for any setting, functioning as an atmospheric background or as accompaniment under the chuppah or centre of the stage on the dance-floor.

We usually operate as a trio (piano, drum kit, saxophone). We can also play as a duo (piano and saxophone) or as a quartet (piano, bass, drum kit and tenor saxophone). We also offer the possibility of a singer. This flexibility of line up gives our customers a range of choice of both format and price. 

The core members of the band have all studied their instruments at world-class conservatoires including the Royal Academy of Music and the Guildhall School of Music and Drama.

Get in contact with the Solomons-Wise Simcha band to bring your Jewish event to life!

column41: Hi there, I want to work towards being a dep bass player for your roster of bands.  I'm 42 live in SW London, have been playing for over 25 years and played extensively in function bands when I was a full time pro musician between 2001 and 2007 doing some very high profile gigs which included corporate events at Disneyland Paris and a very high profile wedding for the Jordanian Royal Family in 2004 when Jordan's Crown Prince Hamzah Bin Al-Hussein and Princess Noor Hamzah were married at the Four Seasons Hotel in Amman.  I was also in the resident band at the Goodwood Festival of Speed between 2002 and 2004.  I attended the Academy of Contemporary Music in Guildford where I studied bass to a Higher Diploma level.  After doing hundreds of function gigs as well as recording sessions for BBC radio and TV, playing at the O2 Wireless Festival in 2006, Guilfest in 2005 etc. I had a career change in 2007 but have continued to work as a session musician on the side.  Now several years on after having children and working in non-music related industries I'm looking to get back into playing live (I used to absolutely love doing function gigs!), and as a first step in working towards being a dep for one of your bands I wanted to register my interest as I work towards relearning the main sets, as well as the newer songs in your bands repertoires.  At this stage I was thinking if you could share some of your bands standard setlists I can get to work on learning the songs again with a view to being ready to dep in the next few months, perhaps by the time Christmas gigs come around.  I don't have social media profiles as I do most of my session work word of mouth but I do have a profile on Airgigs which I've included.  I've recently done some session work with a producer over in Nashville on there where I'm playing with Emmy Lou Harris's red dirt boys, which is my recent claim to fame!   Oh finally!  I have my own transport and pro gear - I have a 1977 Music Man Stingray bass as my main bass, a Fender Jazz as backup, and a Stingray 5 string.  For my amp I use a Mark Bass CMD 102P 2 x 10 combo which is light and compact but perfect for function gigs.  Anyway, I look forward to hearing from you and hopefully doing some dep work for you guys at some point down the line, maybe even joining one of the bands one day all things going well!

Natalie Grace is a London-born singer, songwriter and performer whose upbringing has shaped her music. Being brought up by two musicians who thrived in the 80s music scene, her love of soul music started early.

From age 15, Natalie Grace has been the backing vocalist for 80’s Latin Pop band Modern Romance, who had hits from 1981 onwards, such as Best Years of Our Lives and Everybody Salsa. She performs with them at venues large and small across England, Ireland, Scotland and Wales, including major festivals like Let’s Rock, Rewind Festival and Sunshine Fest. In the summer of 2019, she had the incredible opportunity to perform with the band at Chilfest, with a lineup that included the likes of Jocelyn Brown and Soul ll Soul.

At eleven, Ethan's love of the bass guitar was ignited.  Propelled by his fascination with funk, rock, jazz, and pop music, Ethan has spent 15 years honing his skills as a bass player and excelling in advanced playing techniques, sight-reading, performing abilities, and genre versatility.

He has been performing on-stage for over a decade with numerous bands, from a 'functions' set to a jazz orchestra.  In 2020, he was awarded a First Class in his BA(Hons) degree in Popular Music Performance and Production at Solent University.

Between 2021-2023, he was hired as a bassist for a 90s tribute named The Britpop Band.  With them, he has performed at popular events, such as the Dorchester Cider Festival, Poole Sea Festival, and has even supported the one and only, Chesney Hawkes.

Ethan currently plays bass and is co-vocalist with a heavy rock/pop originals band called NAEMA.  They are releasing new songs on various popular music platforms, and have a lineup of shows that will take them into the new year.  He is also a trusted session bassist for Pure Entertainment and has played numerous tribute/party gigs through them for nearly 3 years.

Ethan has gained valuable experience and performed in multiple festivals including Call of the Wild (Lincolnshire), RagCity2 (Fordingbridge), Sea-Fest (Poole Quay), and many others.  His proudest moment was performing at the 2023 Undercover Tribute festival in Warsaw, Poland - televised with an estimated 3 million viewers!

column38:
Oren Valek is a freelance guitarist and composer based in North London. 

Oren's obsession with music grew out of a love for guitarists Mark Knopfler, David Gilmour, and Eric Clapton. He developed his playing by performing at live venues and open mic sessions from the age of 14. His exposure to live music has given his rock, funk, blues, and soul playing a natural style focused on tone, subtlety, time, and space. 

Throughout his career, Oren has fostered connections with the blues community, such as Samuel Ashton and The Voice finalist Hannah Williams, and regularly features in significant festivals like Broadstairs Blues Bash, Herne Bay Rocks, and City Sound Project. 

At the University of Southampton, he obtained a bachelor’s and master’s degree in guitar performance and composition. Throughout his studies, Oren expanded into neo-soul, gospel, and jazz, becoming a versatile session player, arranger, bandleader, and improviser.
 
For his master’s in composition, Oren focused on creating music that integrates sound design with guitar sounds and orchestral textures. His work is described as a mixture of blues-rock, cinematic, electronic, and minimalistic soundscapes. The success of his projects has led to achieving a distinction for his masters, as well as professional collaborations with Green-Shepherd Saxophone duo, Plus-minus ensemble, and London Southbank University.

Oren's musical story is unique, that establishes a composer and guitarist that is dedicated to his craft.

column38:
Music has always been my greatest passion, and it’s what drives me to create unique live experiences for every audience I play for. As a solo acoustic and electric artist, I love blending classic and contemporary sounds, bringing them to life through looping technology. This allows me to build rich layers of sound in real time, making my performances dynamic and tailored to the mood of the moment. Whether you’re looking for something laid-back and intimate or upbeat and energetic, I can adapt to create exactly the atmosphere you want for your event.

My journey as a musician began with a deep love for guitar and songwriting. Over the years, I've honed my craft, not only through extensive practice but also by performing at a variety of venues and events, each experience shaping the way I connect with audiences. I’ve had the privilege of performing at key venues across London, including my recent set at Ealing Broadway, and have worked with a range of clients seeking music that’s both heartfelt and engaging.

One of the things that sets me apart is my versatility. I can perform solo with acoustic or electric guitar, integrating looping techniques to build full, immersive soundscapes, or I can bring in a full band if your event calls for it. This flexibility means you can tailor the musical setup to your specific needs, whether it's a low-key acoustic set or a larger, high-energy performance.

Over the years, I’ve performed at everything from intimate weddings and private parties to larger events and festivals. I’m comfortable engaging crowds of all sizes, and my goal is always to create a memorable experience for your guests.

Professionalism is at the heart of everything I do. I take pride in being punctual, prepared, and easy to work with, ensuring that the music is one less thing for you to worry about on your big day. My equipment is top-notch, and I’m fully self-sufficient, which means you can count on high-quality sound, no matter the size or location of your event.

Whether you're looking for someone to provide the perfect background music, a lively set that gets everyone on the dance floor, or something in between, I'm confident that I can deliver exactly what you need. If you want a solo performer who can create a big sound with live looping or a full band to bring the energy, I’ll work closely with you to make sure your event hits the right notes.

Let’s make your event unforgettable!

Tom Reynolds is a Brighton based session guitarist specialising in working closely with artists and producers to write emotive, suitable parts and create memorable hooks for their tracks. He is predominantly a blues player but draws inspiration from Indie-Rock, Soul, Funk, R&B, Pop and Folk which ensures a good level of versatility and creativity which comes through in his playing and composing.

He has a wealth of professional experience both performing in live settings with various bands and studio sessions having to write and record parts on the spot. His highlights include a headline tour with britpop band My Life Story in 2024 including two sold out shows at Oxford Street's infamous 100 Club and headlining Popfest in Cologne. Recently he travelled to Austin, TX to play multiple sets at SXSW with his latest Dream - Pop project, Korda Korder, throughout the festival. Korda Korder's first two singles have been played multiple times on BBC Radio 6 Music by Steve Lamacq and on Radio X by John Kennedy. He continues to work with various artists and bands playing many different styles and crafting well thought out, developed parts. In 2023 he was asked by renowned producer Pete Smith to spend three days recording at Homeward Studio in Hove to assist a BIMM masters graduate with recording three original tracks. Function work also remains an important part of his career with an abundance of professional experience playing with high class wedding bands such as The Itchy Feet House Band.

column38:
I am a versatile and experienced musician always keen to take on new projects. 

As a teen I was competent in saxophone, guitar and keyboard (with a bit of drum kit on the side). Primarily self-taught in electric bass, I was accepted into a degree in music performance at London’s ICMP in 2008, where I trained with some of the UK’s top session bassists.

Over the years I have been a member of several busy function bands, specialising in 70s disco, Motown, Memphis soul, rock and 80s to present day party pop, as well as jazz ensembles and local orchestras. These have been often as a bassist, but also in horn sections. I have also been a private instrument tutor, and a music lecturer at Hertford Regional College. Last year I completed an MA degree in music focusing on arranging, composing and performance.

Since November 2021 I have worked contracts on P&O cruise ships as a house band bassist, which required performing several themed sets every week and backing guest artists. One contract had me as bandleader, running the house band for Christmas and new year cruises.

I have played in musical theatre shows including “And the World Goes Round”, “Slice of Saturday Night”, “RENT” and "American Idiot". Besides regular band work, I often dep for bands around the south east, including The Real Deal Collective and tribute act Born Gaga. 

I am a regular bassist for Forever Whitney, Forever Donna Summer, and The Affinity Soul Band (with versatile tribute artist Destiny Michelle), performing at holiday parks, hotels and festivals. More recently I have been performing solo saxophone, specialising in house covers of classic soul and disco.

I relish all of styles, but have a particular love of the sounds of James Jamerson (classic Motown), Rodgers and Edwards (Chic), Thundercat, Bjork, and Prince.
column41: I have also completed an extensive course in live sound engineering, and I am well experienced in setting up sound and light equipment.

Kristy Keller is a dynamic and soulful vocalist whose voice blends timeless jazz elegance with modern charm. With a background in jazz, blues, and soul, Kristy has been captivating audiences for years with her warm tone, rich storytelling, and charismatic stage presence.

She spent five years fronting a 20-piece soul band, bringing powerful, high-energy performances to stages across the region. Kristy now regularly performs with a classic big band in Gloucestershire, where she delivers swinging standards, lush ballads, and toe-tapping showstoppers in true golden-era style.

Whether reimagining 90s and 2000s hits with her duo The Roaring Noughties or curating themed shows for care homes and special events, Kristy brings authenticity, heart, and a deep love of music to every performance. Inspired by legends like Ella Fitzgerald, Billie Holiday, and Amy Winehouse, she offers a vast and versatile repertoire with a contemporary twist.

From theatre lounges and weddings to garden parties and community concerts, Kristy creates unforgettable musical moments for audiences of all ages.

column38: Cassandra Mathews studied classical guitar at the Royal College of Music with Gary Ryan where she obtained a 1st Class Honours Bachelor of Music. She also received coaching from Chris Stell and extra lessons with Craig Ogden and Jakob Lindberg on the lute. She began her studies with John Draper from the age of twelve. During her time at the RCM, she took part in masterclasses with John Williams, Carlos Bonell, Jason Vieaux, and Julian Byzantine as well as performing at Queen Elisabeth Hall and the Purcell Room as part of “The Rest Is Noise” Festival 2013. She has also given chamber music performances at the Wigmore Hall, V&A Museum the London Film Museum. An event in April 2016 took Cassandra to the Instituto Cervantes, where she performed a selection of pieces by celebrated guitar composer Joaquin Rodrigo to his daughter Cecilia Rodrigo. She has also commissioned and premiered a number of works for guitar, violin and shakuhachi as a member of Illuminate Women’s Music in their 2018/2019 season. Cassandra is particularly interested in collaborations with other artistic disciplines and took part in unveiling “Desvalidas” as part of the RCM Great Exhibitionists 2014 working with award-winning poet Isabel White and visual artist Dylan J Fox. She was the winner of the Lennox Berkeley Guitar Prize 2012 and was the given a Highly Commended Award for the annual Royal College of Music Guitar Competition 2014 and 2012. Cassandra represented the RCM at the Hochschule für Musik und Darstellende Kunst in Stuttgart in an Erasmus exchange programme in 2012. She has also played in ensembles as part of the London Guitar Festival 2011 at King's Place and as part of the RCM Symphony Orchestra under Bernard Haitink. Most recently, Cassandra has been performing worldwide on luxury cruise liners, at 5 star resorts internationally and at recitals throughout the UK.

I'm a 41 year old guitarist from near York.  25+ years of live playing in various bands.  Mostly function bands although these days I do a lot of work with tribute bands (Queen, Phil Collins are the current main ones)  I also teach guitar in term-time 3 days a week (Tues-Thurs) but this is always moveable if need for a gig.

I've tons of function band experience.  Easily 1000+ gigs.  So I'm well used to everything it takes to do the job properly.
- Learning songs correctly and to a high standard, so no rehearsal needed.
- Arriving on time
- Set up and pack down quickly and with as little disruption to the event as possible
- I always use amp sims and in-ears so volume is never a problem
- dressing appropriately for the event

column38:
Over the years I have performed in important venues and festivals in Italy, France, Spain, The Netherlands, Germany, United States, Slovenia, Croatia, Latvia and Bosnia both as a band leader and as a sideman. I have taken part in many recording sessions again both as a leader and composer of the music in question and as a sideman. In 2017 I was granted a scholarship to study at Maastricht Conservatorium in the Netherlands where I had the opportunity to specialize in Jazz Music, ensemble playing and improvisation with some of the leading musicians and teachers in Europe and the world such as Joachim Shoenecker and John Goldsby. Thanks to this opportunity I was able to record my first work as a leader ad composer with the band Estia that I formed during my time there. I’ve continued my work as a composer and recorded a series of videos with my music for quintet that have been released as singles featuring some of the most promising young musicians on the Italian scene such as Giovanni Cigui and Emanuele Filippi. During the summer of 2022 I formed a new project called “Cucumber Green”, a duo with Paris-based bassist Eugenio Dreas with whom I recorded my first publication: the EP “Cucumber green” is available on all digital platforms and has been received very positively by national newspaper music critics.
I hold a Bachelor and Master’s degree in Jazz Guitar for which I obtained with top marks. During my time at the Conservatorio J. Tomadini in Udine (Italy) I studied with ECM recording artist and Grammy nominated Glauco Venier, Alfonso Deidda and Alessandro Florio among others. I'm am currently a PhD student at Trinity Laban Conservatoire of Music and Dance doing research in the music creative practice field.
I was able to follow many masterclasses including Kurt Rosenwinkel, Steve Swallow and Adam Nussbaum, Gilad Hekselman, Melissa Aldana, John Clayton, Yotam Silberstain and and I’ve been mentored by world class guitarists and educators such as Romain Pilon, Federico Casagrande, Eran Har Even and John Stowell who I’ve also performed with. In 2021 I was awarded the “Tomorrow’s Jazz” prize for young musicians who are considered the most promising jazz musicians of tomorrow after having competed in the final in the Fenice theatre in Venice. I have been selected as finalist in many national and international Jazz Music competitions such as “Premio Nazionale delle Arti”, “Vittoria Jazz Festival”, “Chicco Bettinardi” and “Premio Marco Tamburini” where I was awarded third prize.

MR V – Vlatko Ivanov
Saxophonist | DJ & SAX Performer | Live Entertainer

Vlatko Ivanov, known on stage as MR V, is a dynamic and versatile performer who seamlessly blends the soulful sounds of the saxophone with the high-energy pulse of DJ sets. Based in the UK, MR V has carved out a unique niche as a DJ & SAX artist, captivating audiences with his electrifying live performances that fuse jazz, house, funk, and chart-topping hits.

A classically trained saxophonist with a flair for improvisation, Vlatko brings a rich musicality to every event—whether it's a luxury wedding, corporate celebration, or high-end club night. His signature setup includes a Selmer Alto Saxophone, Shure wireless systems, and a Pioneer DJ controller, all powered through a premium Electro-Voice Evolve 50 PA system and enhanced by a stunning Equinox DJ booth and intelligent lighting.

With years of experience performing across Europe and the UK, MR V is known for his ability to read the crowd, elevate the atmosphere, and deliver unforgettable musical moments. Whether he's serenading guests during a sunset cocktail hour or igniting the dance floor with a sax-infused DJ set, MR V brings passion, professionalism, and pure entertainment.

column38:
Ambitious, diverse and heavily swayed by extraordinary sound, Fred Hills creates captivating compositions by fusing influences from around the musical world. Inspired to play by the unique and revolutionary musicians that preceded him, Fred Hills incorporates his love of jazz, prog, electronica, world and folk music into his colourful rhythms, melodic ‘open-handed’ beats and delicate guitar-based compositions. Taking inspiration from a wide range of genres, Hills' music is a captivating blend of contemporary jazz, alt-folk & progressive indie.

Fred's debut EP 'Incerto', released via Ninja Tune, draws influences from The Cinematic Orchestra, White Denim, Jose Gonzales and others. BBC Introducing described Hills' EP as, ‘a tapestry of delicate and expressive guitars, eloquent strings and colourful rhythms’. This was brought to life through collaborative efforts with producers and sound designers; Alex Barron and Aneek Thapar, who share Fred’s vision to create music and sounds that defy genres and cross traditional boundaries.

Hills' versatility as a musician is evident in his collaborations with a variety of artists, including Ellie Ford, Michael Baker, Time for T and Mara Simpson. He has also lent his compositional talents to theatre, creating music for award-winning play 'YOU' by Mark Wilson and 'Something Underground's' acclaimed 'Knife on The Table'. 

Pivotal release 'The Ground', is a fine example of Fred's ability to diversify his sound, trading his folk sensibilities for dynamic lyricism, expansive production and punchy drum grooves. Fred regularly performs with his trio, which consists of multi-instrumentalist & arranger Andrew Stuart-Buttle (Lucy Rose, Bess Atwell, Samantha Crain) and double bassist Alfie Weedon.

column38: Davide Di Camillo is an accomplished Italian bass player, composer, and educator. He pursued his studies at the Conservatory L. D’Annunzio in Pescara, where he obtained a bachelor’s degree in electric bass with a focus on popular music. He subsequently earned a master’s degree in electric bass with an emphasis on jazz, studying under the esteemed Prof. Maurizio Rolli, known for his work with artists such as Vince Mendoza, Mike Stern, Dave Weckl, and Hiram Bullock. Davide further honed his craft under the mentorship of renowned bassist Sharay Reed, who has collaborated with notable figures including Aretha Franklin, Chaka Khan, Yolanda Adams, Kirk Franklin, and Cory Henry. With over a decade of live performance and studio session experience, Davide has participated in a diverse array of musical projects spanning genres from pop to jazz to metal. His collaborative efforts include working with international musicians such as Mike Applebaum, Fabrizio Bosso, Francesco Mendolia, Maurizio Rolli, Vince Tempera, Massimo Morganti, and Claudio Filippini. Currently, he is engaged as a music transcriber for the forthcoming Sharay Reed bass transcriptions book. In 2022, he also collaborated with Reed as a transcriber for the “Bach n Bass” transcription book. In 2021, Davide released his second album as a composer, leader, and bassist titled *PATCHWORK - Davide Di Camillo*. His first album, *Farina Del Mio Sacco*, was released in 2018 under Dodicilune Records, showcasing his talents as a leader, bassist, and composer. His achievements include receiving the second prize at the International Festival of Conservatories in Frosinone in 2017 with the band F-Army, as well as the first prize at the National Festival of Conservatories in Frosinone in 2016 as the band leader, bassist, and composer with the Glutenfree Trio. The Glutenfree Trio was invited to perform in Istanbul by the Italian National Music Committee (CIDIM) and at the Pescara Jazz Festival. Additionally, he has recorded the album *A Christmas Carol* with Choruscant and the first album of F-Army, titled *Call to Arms*.

column38:
My name is Peter Kos, I live in Aylesbury (Buckinghamshire), I've been playing bass for over twenty years and am a well experienced, proficient bass player. I've played in numerous bands and on many stages and am familiar with many genres.

The bands I have played with (UK ones only):
- Freight Train Riders / Leighton Buzzard (still with them for over 3 years)
- Joyrider function band / Letchworth (depping for 3 months)
- Ripple Effect party band / Luton (recurring depping role)
- Afterlife Rock Covers Band UK / Harlow (dep role)
- MKCC / Milton Keynes (church bassist for over 2 years)

I do not read sheet music, but play everything by ear even on the fly. I am a confident, technically skilled player who can play even complex and challenging bass lines or parts if needed, and always bring the 'factory' (or very close!) bass line; that is I am not lazy to learn, to invest time and energy to sound as close as possible to the original bass lines, including sound characteristics. (A Motown song's bass line, for example, features a completely different sound character than a modern pop or rock song, or numbers that have a synth bass line (played on keyboard), which I also can provide; playing on bass but sounding like a synthesizer.)

I am a very quick and effective learner, having - it looks like - an extremely good memory to pick up songs or even complete set lists, that is crucial especially for short-notice depping requests. Here is a list that demonstrates my learning ability:
- Joyrider Function Band - 38 songs from scratch in a week
- Ripple Effect Party Band - 22 songs from scratch in a week
- Afterlife Rock Covers Band UK - 25 songs from scratch in two weeks

All of these meant to be stage quality, playing them live - without any rehearsals with the above bands, when stepped in as a dep. bassist.

I am a team player who knows exactly that there is no good vibe, even good sounding as a band without being attentive, communicative, helpful and flexible! If a band has no proper 'chemistry' (coming from a great team spirit) it will barely be able to get people dancing and enjoying the event. My rich experience has taught me how to be a team member, not a 'soloist' or a 'maverick'. I consider myself a calm, composed character who is happy to help or work closely with others. 

I have quality instruments and a pro gear (including IEMs) and my own transportation as well.

Known for professionalism, adaptability, and a collaborative approach, Robbie brings precision, groove, and a positive musical energy to every project — making him a valuable addition to The Supreme Collective’s roster of top-tier musicians.

column38:
Daisy Pepper is a session bassist in Hertfordshire, UK. Confident in performing, recording and touring, Daisy is an experienced and versatile player.
In 2020, Daisy started sharing her musical journey on social media, and since then has gained tens of thousands of followers and millions of views across various platforms. As a result of the fantastic response, Daisy became a full time session bassist.
​
Daisy is an artist with Spector Basses, Ashdown Amps, EMG Pickups, Darkglass Electronics, RightOn! Straps, DR Strings and Treepicks. She currently plays bass in the band 'The Exile Club', with their debut album "Chained To Ruins & Rust" released in September 2024.
 
Daisy has connected with brands and companies such as Aguilar Amps, Fender, Faith Guitars, Shergold, Barnes & Mullins, Gear4Music, Underfluked Pedals, Hartwood Guitars, Moises.ai, Ibanez, Korg USA & UK, Donner Music, Lava Music, Alpher Instruments, Origin Effects, Manson Guitar Works, ENYA Music and Optima Strings for promotional work.
​
From January 2024-2025, Daisy became a coach at Scott's Bass Lessons, hosting weekly livestreams for the SBL LiveClass. Here, she taught and talked about all things rock music. As of September 2024, Daisy is the bass player in 'Hullabaloo - Muse Tribute'.
​
March 2025 was an exciting month for Daisy, starting with a main stage performance at The Guitar Show 2025 in collaboration with Spector and Shergold. Here, she talked about her experience as a session musician, and went on to perform some tracks from The Exile Club. The excitement continued on into April, as Daisy featured on the front page of Bass Review Magazine, which included an exclusive interview discussing all things bass, being a Woman in the industry, and some top tips for the industry.

column38:
CoShaun Evans, known artistically as Cush Evans, is a Bermudian singer, songwriter, and rapper. Born on March 3, 1993, he has been performing since 2001, rapping since 2007, and recording music since 2010. His music has received radio airplay since 2012, and he has been performing internationally since 2012.

Cush Evans’ musical style is diverse, encompassing R&B, Pop, Reggae, Hip Hop, Old Skool, and Soca. He is noted for his vocal range and ability to cover various genres. His work is influenced by Bermudian and Jamaican cultures, as well as the next generation, and he emphasizes real-life lyrics in his music.

Throughout his career, Evans has garnered several accolades and notable performances. He was a two-time winner of BET’s “WildnOut Wednesdays” competition in 2013 and 2014. In Bermuda, he was the runner-up and received the Showmanship Award in Bermuda Idol 2018. He also won the “Quest for the Best” Soca Song Competition in 2017 with his song “Run Road.” In 2022, he was recognized with the Premier’s Award for R&B Singer of the Year.

Evans has performed in various international locations, including New York, Atlanta (Gospel), Jamaica (Acapella), Cayman Islands (Acapella), and St. Vincent (Soca). He has opened for notable artists such as Movado and Bunji Garlin in 2015, and Luciano in 2019. His music has been released on platforms like iTunes, Amazon, and Spotify.

Beyond his music career, CoShaun Evans has also engaged in acting, participating in Troika Bermuda in 2013 and 2014, and Authentic Expressions Jamaica in 2014 and 2015. He has also undertaken modeling work for Gibbons Company Bermuda in 2019.
